resources.title: 100 Things to See in the Night Sky
resources.author: Dean Regas
resources.maySub: 100 Things to See in the Southern Sky, if in the southern hemisphere.
resources.rationale: A conversational guide through Astronomy Field Studies
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: 101 American Geo-Sites You've Gotta See
resources.author: Albert Dickas
resources.maySub: Aerial Geology by Mary Caperton Morton
resources.rationale: Geographical survey of geological sites of interest, includes a well-written geological analysis on each site and excellent coverage across regions of the U.S.  Canadian learners may prefer Aerial Geology.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: 1493 for Young People
resources.author: Charles Mann, Adapted by Rebecca Stefoff
resources.rationale: This book helps students understand both the positive and negative effects that resulted from the Columbian Exchange (the exchange of goods and people between the Old World and the New World during the Age of Exploration) in the 15th century. This thought-provoking book gets past the facts of history to explore the ideas that still affect us today.
resources.note: Make sure to buy the Young Reader's Version. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: 1493: Uncovering the New World Columbus Created
resources.author: Charles Mann
resources.rationale: The story of globalization starting in the 15th century.
resources.note: This book is used in several rotations and is a more advanced version of the book in Grade 9. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: 303 Tween-Approved Exercises and Active Games
resources.author: Kimberly Wechsler
resources.rationale: This book provides a great list of exercises that includes strength training, flexibility exercises, and cardiovascular activities. This can be used in a group or with one student.

resources.title: 50 Adventures in the 50 States
resources.author: Kate Siber
resources.rationale: This beautifully illustrated book provides a two-page spread for each state and shows what activities can be done based on the geographical features of the area. This book will be used alongside Cross Country to follow the Page family as they travel across the country.

resources.title: 50 Buildings You Should Know
resources.author: Isabel Kuhl
resources.rationale: This book is used for several history rotations to study some of the fascinating buildings of the time period. For this rotation, there are only 2 lessons from this book, so we have made the book optional.  No need to purchase the book just for this rotation.

resources.title: A Brief History of the World in 47 Borders: Surprising Stories Behind the Lines on Our Maps
resources.author: John Elledge
resources.rationale: A collection of interesting border histories and legacies that tie into the history rotations, citizenship discussions, and map making of the previous years.

resources.title: A Child's Introduction to the Orchestra
resources.author: Wilder & Barer
resources.rationale: Creative introduction to each instrument of the orchestra through song.
resources.note: Unavailable for purchase.

resources.title: A Christmas Carol
resources.author: Charles Dickens
resources.rationale: As a famous work of 19th century literature, A Christmas Carol expands students' understanding of this historical period while also nourishing them with a thought-provoking tale about greed and generosity. It is scheduled in the mythology stream as Dicken's work has become a modern myth, shaping our conceptions of Christmas and more.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: A Field Guide to Bacteria
resources.author: Betsy Dexter Dyer
resources.rationale: A fascinating look at the world of bacteria.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: A Fruit is a Suitcase for Seeds
resources.author: Jean Richards
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book about seeds as preferred by the teacher.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the ways that plants use seeds. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: A History of Canada in Ten Maps: Epic Stories of Charting a Mysterious Land
resources.author: Adam Shoalts
resources.rationale: This book tells the story of the discovery and development of the land that would become Canada by exploring the creation and usage of ten significant maps. It is an innovative and engaging approach to Canadian history, written by an academic and modern-day adventurer who has followed many of the routes taken by the explorers he includes in this book. It shows how history writing can create a more vivid representation of the past when it draws on biography, story-telling, geography, and cartography.
resources.note: This book will be used over two years in high school (Rotation 2 and Rotation 3). In 2026-2027, students will read the second half of the book. Interested students may wish to read the first half over the summer, but it is not necessary since all chapters are mostly self-contained.

resources.title: A History of the World in 100 Objects
resources.author: Neil MacGregor
resources.rationale: Helpful reference to use as a resource for Book of Centuries.

resources.title: A History of US: Liberty for All?: 1820-1860
resources.author: Joy Hakim
resources.rationale: This volume is a great spine that covers the times and events between 1820-1860. Through the use of engaging narrative, photographs and primary sources, Hakim helps students get a feel for the US during a time of westward expansion, railroad building, and industrialization.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: A House Spider's Life
resources.author: John Himmelman
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book about spiders as preferred by the teacher.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common, but often less appreciated, creature. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: A Kick in the Head: An Everyday Guide to Poetic Forms
resources.author: Paul B. Janeczko
resources.rationale: This engaging book of poetry introduces readers to twenty-nine poetic forms with examples from classic and modern poets.

resources.title: A Little Book for New Historians
resources.author: Robert Tracy McKenzie
resources.rationale: Robert Tracy McKenzie offers a concise, clear, and beautifully-written introduction to the study of history.  Loaded with concrete examples and insightful principles, this primer shows how the study of history, faithfully pursued, can shape your heart as well as your mind. Videos of the author discussing his book are found in the lesson plans.
resources.note: Also highly recommended as a resource for parents and teachers.

resources.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
resources.author: William Shakespeare
resources.rationale: One of Shakespeare’s most popular plays, A Midsummer Night’s Dream is a comedy set in Greece during the 12th century. The magical setting of this play is the city of Athens and the nearby enchanted woods where fairies and their mischief reign in the moonlight.
resources.note: This is a good play for those who are newer to Shakespeare.

resources.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream (Audio recording)
resources.author: Arkangel
resources.maySub: Librivox recording.
resources.rationale: Hearing a play being performed often helps students with comprehension. These professional recordings are superb.

resources.title: A Passion for the Impossible: The Life of Lilias Trotter
resources.author: Miriam Rockness
resources.rationale: An inspiring biography of Lilias Trotter, a British artist who forsook fame to devote her life to Christian missionary work in Algeria, guided by faith, creativity, and love.

resources.title: A Peterson Field Guide To Western Trees
resources.author: Olivia Petrides
resources.rationale: Regional guide
resources.note: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

resources.title: A Planet of Viruses
resources.author: Carl Zimmer
resources.rationale: This is an especially relevant book for today.
resources.note:
Choose this book OR Microbe Hunters.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: A Short History of Ancient Rome
resources.author: Pascal Hughes
resources.rationale: This book charts the dramatic twists and turns of the Roman kingdom, the republic, the empire and its decline. Pascal Hughes writes the history in a popular form that is engaging and easy to understand. Students will walk away from the reading with a deep understanding of one of the most powerful nations in the world.
resources.note: The first few chapters contain sensitive content and will not be assigned to students. Next year's ancient history rotation is 100 B.C.-350 A.D. Therefore, students will begin with ch.6 when Sulla becomes dictator.

resources.title: A Simply Healthy Life
resources.author: Caroline Fausel
resources.rationale: Health coach and wellness expert Caroline Fausel introduces her readers to simple strategies for living a healthy life. From fueling our bodies and integrating exercise to using spiritual practices like Sabbath rest and setting up a system for habits, Caroline lays out the steps needed to live a healthy, fulfilled life.

resources.title: A Walk in the...
resources.author: Rebecca L. Johnson
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about a biome (one that is familiar for Term 1; one that is not for Term 3).
resources.rationale: These beautiful picture books take us on a walk through the biomes, introducing the various plants and animals we might encounter in various seasons. They are a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
resources.note:
Links provide various options in this biome book series. Teachers should select one that is familiar to the student for Term 1 and one that will be less familiar for Term 3.

Deciduous Forest is available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: A Wonder Book for Girls and Boys
resources.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
resources.rationale: Nathaniel Hawthorne is a master storyteller. This collection of stories from Greek mythology is a great introduction for young children.
resources.note: Grade 3 students should read EITHER Pilgrim's Progress OR A Wonder Book.

resources.title: A World in a Drop of Water: Exploring with a Microscope
resources.author: Alvin and Virginia Silverstein
resources.rationale: This book introduces students to the microscopic creatures that live in natural water. They will examine pond water under a microscope to see the hidden lives of single-celled organisms.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: A World of Poetry: Student Book
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: This volume helps students understand the elements of poetry that poets use to create meaning and beautiful language. Poetic devices are examined through the reading of classic and modern poems.
resources.note: You do not need to purchase the Teacher's Manual. We will provide extra support and answers to the exercises in the lesson plans.

resources.title: A Year in the South: 1865
resources.author: Stephen Ash
resources.rationale: This true, nonfiction account uses letters and diaries to tell the stories of four different people over the course of 1865–both during and right after the Civil War ends. It gives the student a look into the lives of people who were maneuvering through a tumultuous change. It encourages students to see that Reconstruction was a complex time that didn’t mean instant equality or peace.
resources.note: The students will not read all four stories.

resources.title: Abraham Lincoln and Frederick Douglass: The Story Behind and American Friendship
resources.author: Russell Freedman
resources.rationale: This book explores the Civil War era through the evolving relationship of these two highly influential men. It is a fascinating look into how real people grappled with the moral issues of the day.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Abraham Lincoln: An Initial Biography
resources.author: Genevieve Foster
resources.rationale: This classic biography is back in print! Foster presents the life of Abraham Lincoln through an engaging narrative that starts with his humble beginnings and takes the reader through his political rise, his presidency during the Civil War, and his assassination and legacy.
resources.note: Please use the Option 1 link to utilize the discount code.

resources.title: Across This Land
resources.author: John C Hudson
resources.maySub: 1st edition but some information--especially regarding natural resources--will be out of date.
resources.rationale: This book provides a narrative regional study of North America for our high school students.
resources.note: Used across multiple years. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Adoniram Judson: Danger on the Streets of Gold
resources.author: Irene Howat
resources.rationale: The first missionary ever sent from America, Adoniram Judson (1788-1850), faced many challenges as he ministered in the country of Burma. Although he faced many personal trials and even imprisonment by the government, he rejoiced that his translation work and ministry to individuals reached people with the gospel of Jesus Christ.
resources.note: Please use Option 1 to utilize the discount code.

resources.title: Advanced Academic Writing II Teacher Manual
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: Advanced Academic Writing II helps students learn the basics of source integration, literary analysis, academic style, and the writing process. Only the teacher manual is required, as it includes the full text of the student book plus special boxes that contain extra tips and questions that students and instructors will reference.

resources.title: Aesop's Favorite Fables
resources.author: Aesop, illus. by Milo Winter
resources.maySub: another collection of Aesop's Fables. Lesson plans include the story titles.
resources.rationale: Aesop's Fables have made learning life's lessons enjoyable for 2500 years. The illustrations by Milo Winter set this collection apart.

resources.title: Africa Is Not a Country
resources.author: Margy Burns Knight
resources.rationale: This nonfiction picture book introduces students to the diversity of the African continent. Using photographs and short descriptions, it shows students that Africa is made up of many different countries, cultures, languages, landscapes, and ways of life. It will be read slowly throughout the year to help students get a better picture of modern-day Africa.

resources.title: Algebra 2/Trig Ancillary Materials
resources.author: Math Without Borders
resources.rationale: Solutions manual for Algebra 2/Trig

resources.title: Algebra and Trigonometry: Functions and Applications
resources.author: Paul Foerster
resources.maySub: earlier edition or online course with derekowens.com.
resources.rationale: A master teacher guides you through the next level of algebra and trig.
resources.note: Choose this or Denison.

resources.title: All Over the Map: A Cartographic Odyssey
resources.author: Betsy Mason & Greg Miller
resources.rationale: A collection of amazing maps covering a wide range of topics.  Students will be inspired to make maps of their own.
resources.note: Used over several years.

resources.title: Alveary Biology Lab Book
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This document contains all of the instructions, procedures, and guidance needed to complete the laboratory component of High School Biology.
resources.note: Relevant supplies from the Grade 10 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.

resources.title: Alveary Chemistry Lab Book
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This document contains all of the instructions, procedures, and guidance needed to complete the laboratory component of High School Chemistry.
resources.note: Relevant supplies from the Grade 9 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.

resources.title: Alveary Exclusive Cambridge Latin Unit 1 Online Course
resources.author: Angela Reed
resources.rationale: Video lessons taught by a master teacher.
resources.note:
Students receive a year of access from the time they login to the course on our online platform.

Materials needed: Student book

resources.title: Alveary Exclusive Cambridge Latin Unit 2 Online Course
resources.author: Angela Reed
resources.rationale: Video course taught by a master teacher in our Alveary course platform.
resources.note:
Students will receive a year of access to our online platform starting Aug 1st, and lessons will be released throughout the year. Click the link for details on the course calendar, samples, and more.

Materials needed: Student book

resources.title: American History, Volume 1: 1492-1877
resources.author: Thomas Kidd
resources.rationale: In American History: Volumes 1 and 2, Thomas Kidd tells the story of the United States from early American encounters to the modern-day American culture in an engaging and fresh way. Thomas Kidd uses primary sources and other scholarly resources to introduce the student to the people and places that have great historical significance—whether good or bad.

resources.title: American History, Volume 2: 1877 - Present
resources.author: Thomas Kidd
resources.rationale: In American History: Volumes 1 and 2, Thomas Kidd tells the story of the United States from early American encounters to the modern-day American culture in an engaging and fresh way. Thomas Kidd uses primary sources and other scholarly resources to introduce the student to the people and places that have great historical significance—whether good or bad.

resources.title: American Literature
resources.author: William Long
resources.rationale: A helpful chronological look at American Literature and authors.
resources.note: Selections used across multiple rotations.

resources.title: American National Writers: Irving, Simms, Kennedy, Harper, Hawthorne, Harte, Twain
resources.author: Various Writers
resources.note: PDFs included in the Lesson plans.

resources.title: American Tall Tales
resources.author: Mary Pope Osborne
resources.rationale: These larger-than-life stories capture something of America's personality. Mary Pope Osborne has retold these stories well, and the wood-carved illustrations are delightful.
resources.note: U.S. Student Option. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: An Honorable Heritage: The Pandita Ramabai Story in Her Own Words
resources.author: Pandita Ramabai
resources.rationale: Pandita Ramabai (1858-1922) was a social reformer, an advocate for the emancipation of women, and a pioneer in education. She tells her testimony of being born into a privileged Brahman caste in India and leaving it all behind to become a Christian. It is a fascinating read!
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.

resources.title: Ancient Empires: From Mesopotamia to the Rise of Islam
resources.author: Eric H. Cline & Mark W. Graham
resources.rationale: Ancient Empires is a relatively brief yet comprehensive and even-handed overview of the ancient Near East, the Mediterranean, and Europe, including the Greco-Roman world, Late Antiquity, and the early Muslim period.
resources.note: Used for several rotations.

resources.title: Annals of the Former World
resources.author: John McPhee
resources.rationale: Like the terrain it covers, Annals of the Former World tells a multilayered tale, and the reader may choose one of many paths through it. This is a long book, which students may or may not finish, depending on interest.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: Around the World in 80 Tales
resources.author: Saviour Pirotta
resources.rationale: This book takes readers across six continents with short, entertaining folktales to stir our first graders' imaginations with tales from many lands.

resources.title: Art Print Set
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This art print set contains high-resolution images that are in the public domain or used for Alveary with permission. The Art Print Set contains paintings by each term's artist: Edward Mitchell Bannister, Vincent van Gogh, and Lilias Trotter. We recommend printing on cardstock. Hard copies will be available with lesson plans.
resources.note: Selections are also included in Our Work packets for Grades 1-12.

resources.title: Astronomy Textbook
resources.author: Charlesworth, Castro, & Delgado
resources.rationale: This general astronomy text includes a brief history of astronomy, as well as fundamentals of the field.  It is a well-organized and concise text written from the perspective that faith and science are compatible.

resources.title: Atomic Habits
resources.author: James Clear
resources.rationale: James Clear, one of the world's leading experts on habit formation, reveals practical strategies that will teach you exactly how to form good habits, break bad ones, and master the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results. This book brings Mason's thoughts on habits into the 21st century applying current research in this field.
resources.note: Choose this OR Think

resources.title: Baking Class
resources.author: Deanna Cook
resources.rationale: This book teaches the secrets of great baking, using kid-friendly directions and recipes.

resources.title: Bat Loves the Night
resources.author: Nicola Davies
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book about bats as preferred by the teacher.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common but less-often-observed creature. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: Becoming by Beholding
resources.author: Lanta Davis
resources.rationale: This book introduces and leads students to experience how God can use our imaginations for our spiritual formation.  No matter what church tradition you are a part of, there are helpful ideas and practices here to grow a student's relationship with God for a lifetime.

resources.title: Beetle Busters
resources.author: Loree Griffin Burns
resources.rationale: The Asian longhorned beetle has devastated forest ecosystems in America. Students are introduced to basic forestry as they learn about the problem of invasive species. The author beautifully invites the reader to consider the challenges of dealing with this complex issue and encourages dialogue and collaboration as part of the problem-solving process.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: Being God's Image: Why Creation Still Matters
resources.author: Carmen Joy Imes
resources.rationale: Carmen Joy Imes seeks to recover the theologically rich message of the creation narratives starting in the book of Genesis as they illuminate what it means to be human.

resources.title: Belle of Batoche
resources.author: Jacqueline Guest
resources.rationale: Belle of Batoche is a work of historical fiction which imagines one girl's experience in the Battle of Batoche in 1885, where Métis fighters led by Louis Riel raised an ultimately unsuccessful defence against Canadian militia troops in Batoche, Saskatchewan. A simple story of two girls' rivalry and eventual friendship is woven into an historically-accurate depiction both of life in the late-nineteenth century Canadian prairies and of specific events from the battle itself.
resources.note: This is good as an independent read for Grade 4 or confident readers.

resources.title: Blood and Iron: The Rise and Fall of the German Empire
resources.author: Katja Hoyer
resources.rationale: Katja Hoyer is a German-British historian and expert in the field of Germany’s history. She brings that expertise to the pages of her book which engages with the people, policies, and places that helped build the German Empire and ultimately led to its downfall. This book offers the student the history of a nation whose national pride led to two World Wars.

resources.title: Body into Balance: An Herbal Guide to Holistic Self-Care
resources.author: Maria Groves
resources.rationale: Herbalist Maria Noel Groves opens the door to natural wellness by taking the reader on a journey through the major body systems - respiratory, digestive, immune, nervous, memory, circulatory and more - and showing readers how to support those body systems with natural remedies.

resources.title: Born!: A Foal, Five Kittens and Confederation
resources.author: Deirdre Kessler
resources.rationale: A delightful story about the everyday life of twin siblings in Charlottetown during the Charlottetown Conference of 1864. Readers will learn about everyday life in nineteenth-century British North America as well as the events and political figures who were key to establishing Canada's Confederation.

resources.title: Botany in a Day
resources.author: Thomas Elpel
resources.rationale: A patterned approach to learning the plant families.

resources.title: Brush Drawing as Applied to Natural Forms and Common Objects
resources.author: May Mallam
resources.rationale: This is a complete guide to learning how to brush-draw. Exercises progress from simple lines to monochromatic value painting to painting in color. Techniques taught help students learn to "see" so that they can visually represent their observations.

resources.title: Brushwork: Elementary Brush-Forms
resources.author: Marion Hudson
resources.rationale: This book is perfect for those who are new to brush drawing. The exercises on gridded paper help students learn to mix colors and control the brush. Brushforms ("blobs") are used to create designs.

resources.title: Bryson's Dictionary of Troublesome Words: A Writer's Guide to Getting It Right
resources.author: Bill Bryson
resources.rationale: A helpful resource for avoiding error and increasing clarity in composition.

resources.title: Buffalo Bird Girl: A Hidatsa Story
resources.author: S. D. Nelson
resources.rationale: This book lets students walk in the shoes of a Native American girl. It is based on the real childhood experiences of a Hidatsa woman born around 1839 who lived along the Missouri River on the Great Plains. Students are introduced to the tribe's customs and daily routines, their relationships with other tribes, and their initial interactions with the white man. The story also tells how the Hidatsa way of life was changed forever when they were forced to relocate to a reservation.

resources.title: Buzztail and Leaper
resources.author: Robert M. McClung
resources.rationale: Two stories in one, this classic presents two very different animals in the context of their ecosystems. Students read about their habitats, biomes, food chains, life cycles, adaptations, and more.

resources.title: By Design: Ethics, Theology, and the Practice of Engineering
resources.author: Brad J. Kallenberg
resources.rationale: An often overlooked topic, this text challenges students to consider the social context and formative nature of design.

resources.title: Caesar's English II
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: Studies in Latin stems, alongside typical classical and academic vocabulary words, will aid students in using, spelling and understanding these words in context.
resources.note: We strongly recommend the print version of this book since it has extra resources which are sometimes mentioned in the lesson plans, but you can choose paperback or ebook, black & white or color.

resources.title: Cambridge Latin Unit 1 Online Resources
resources.rationale: Cambridge Go is a subscription service that includes a digital version of the student book, audios of the stories, online games, and practice. This is helpful for students who are learning Latin independently.
resources.note: If you are using an older edition of the text that does not have a code in the front of the book, you must email Cambridge in order to purchase Elevate/Cambridge Go. [cambridgeelevatehelp.cambridge.org/hc/en-gb/requests/ne](https://cambridgeelevatehelp.cambridge.org/hc/en-gb/requests/new)w

resources.title: Cambridge Latin Unit 2 Online Resources
resources.rationale: Elevate is a subscription service that includes a digital version of the student book, audios of the stories, online games, and practice. This is helpful for students who are learning Latin independently.
resources.note: If you are using an older edition of the text that does not have a code in the front of the book, you must email Cambridge in order to purchase Elevate/Cambridge Go. [cambridgeelevatehelp.cambridge.org/hc/en-gb/requests/ne](https://cambridgeelevatehelp.cambridge.org/hc/en-gb/requests/new)w

resources.title: Cambridge Latin Unit 3 Online Resources
resources.author: Cambridge School Classics Project
resources.rationale: Elevate is a subscription service that includes a digital version of the student book, audios of the stories, online games, and practice. This is helpful for students who are learning Latin independently.
resources.note:
If you are using an older edition of the text that does not have a code in the front of the book, you must email Cambridge in order to purchase Elevate/Cambridge Go. [cambridgeelevatehelp.cambridge.org/hc/en-gb/requests/ne](https://cambridgeelevatehelp.cambridge.org/hc/en-gb/requests/new)w
Option 2 links to the book that includes digital access to the online resources.

resources.title: Cambridge Latin Unit 4 5th Ed.
resources.author: Cambridge School Classics Project
resources.rationale: This is the student book. Includes a selection of original texts by Roman authors (adapted where necessary), including Catullus, Ovid, Pliny, Tacitus, and Virgil.
resources.note: This edition comes with 1-year access to Elevate/Cambridge Go.

resources.title: Cambridge Latin Unit 4 Online Resources
resources.rationale: Site contains free cultural background information. Elevate/Cambridge Go is a subscription service for online games and practice.
resources.note: The student book we link includes 1-year access to Elevate/Cambridge Go.

resources.title: Cambridge Latin Units 1-4 Teacher Manual (1 Year Digital Access)
resources.author: Cambridge School Classics Project
resources.rationale: Contains important teaching tips.
resources.note: Students using Unit 1 Companion course by Alveary do not need this book. Please call the Cambridge University Press Customer Service team at 1-800-872-7423 (toll-free) or email them at usschools@cambridge.org to purchase. There is also a 4 Year Digital Access option with ISBN 9781316640524.

resources.title: Canada Wild: Animals Found Nowhere Else on Earth
resources.author: Maria Birmingham
resources.rationale: This colourful, illustrated guide to Canada's endemic species introduces young readers to twelve special animals that can only be found in Canada and who have much to teach us about the varied ecosystems that make up our country.

resources.title: Canada's Constitutional Monarchy
resources.author: Nathan Tidridge
resources.rationale: Brimming with photographs and archival images, this book, written by a high school history teacher, is an accessible and extensive account of the role played by the Crown in Canada's history, Constitution, system of parliamentary government, other institutions, and the day-to-day lives of all Canadians.
resources.note: This book is used over two years (Grades 8 and 9).

resources.title: Canadian Student Atlas
resources.author: Collins Maps
resources.maySub: other atlas; however, map work and other lessons refer specifically to this one.
resources.rationale: Used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.
resources.note: For Canadian students only. Optional for Grade 1.

resources.title: Canadian Wall Map (CA students only)
resources.author: National Geographic
resources.maySub: any Canadian wall map that shows both geographical features and place names. Avoid maps with roadways, as these obscure other features.
resources.rationale: This map clearly shows landforms, and its projection is more accurate than some other maps.
resources.note: Hang map at student eye level.

resources.title: Caring for Words in a Culture of Lies (2nd ed.)
resources.author: Marilyn McEntyre
resources.rationale: Thoughtful and relevant ideas on writing for today's students.

resources.title: Celtic Folklore Cooking
resources.author: Joanne Asala
resources.maySub: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture
resources.note: Choose this OR Mexico in Your Kitchen, Classic Russian Cooking, Pomp and Sustenance, Gullah Geechee Home Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing

resources.title: Charlotte's Web
resources.author: E.B. White
resources.rationale: The classic tale is about a spider, a pig, and much more. This book was chosen not only for its enduring literary quality but also to provide opportunities in their lessons for young readers to read books themselves.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Children of the Wild West
resources.author: Russell Freedman
resources.rationale: The author does an excellent job of showing what it was like for pioneer children to experience life in the 1800's. Many fascinating photographs are included that help students see how different life was back then. He also tells about what life was like for some Native American children who had to navigate boarding schools and give up their traditional ways.
resources.note: Available with Audio on Learning Ally.

resources.title: Children's Book of Art
resources.author: DK
resources.rationale: This is a nice overview of global art history with some different pieces shown and discussed. It also has close-up sections explaining how to do things in art such as mixing colors or making a fresco, etc. This will be referenced throughout the course for different techniques and extra examples of genres. However, this will be a second resource and all topics will be taught in a way that students can see images online if they do not have the book on hand. This book was chosen due to its minuscule amount of nudity compared to other art books geared to teens.

resources.title: Christian Liberty Nature Reader Book 4
resources.author: Edward J. Shewan
resources.rationale: Introduces 21st-century North American students to a variety of common birds, insects, reptiles, and their habits.

resources.title: Christians Reading Classics
resources.author: Nadya Williams
resources.rationale: Christianity began in a pagan land with its own myths and writings about gods, villains, and heroes. These stories would become ingrained in the culture. Stories from Homer and writings from Plato and Aristotle would impact the Greco-Roman world and still impact our world.  Williams explores the importance of understanding the content of these classics and the way they impacted the world, as Christians.
resources.note: Only specific chapters will be assigned.

resources.title: Citizenship Without Illusions: A Christian Guide to Political Engagement
resources.author: David T. Koyzis
resources.rationale: This book provides the reader with insightful ways to participate in the cultural and political landscape without setting aside one's spiritual convictions to do so. Koyzis addresses numerous topics such as voting, community engagement, and working with individuals with vastly different political ideologies. He grounds his approach in the reality that Christians are to love God with their heart, soul, mind, and strength, which results in loving their neighbor as themselves. He draws from decades of research, teaching, and student interactions, all from a global perspective, which makes this book a unique and timely resource.

resources.title: Classic Russian Cooking
resources.author: Elena Molokhovets
resources.maySub: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture
resources.note: Choose this OR Mexico in Your Kitchen, Gullah Geechee Home Cooking, Pomp and Sustenance, Celtic Folklore Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing

resources.title: Coding & Logic STEM Activity Book for Kids
resources.author: Bob Mather
resources.rationale: This book includes hours of education and outrageous fun through puzzles, engaging characters, and beginner coding activities.

resources.title: Coding for Kids: Python: Learn to Code with 50 Awesome Games and Activities
resources.author: Adrienne Tacke
resources.rationale: This book includes games and activities that teach kids to code with Python.

resources.title: Color: A Course in Mastering the Art of Mixing Colors
resources.author: Betty Edwards
resources.rationale: This is an optional resource for students who would like a resource guide for color theory and/or would want to learn more than what will be covered in lessons.

resources.title: Commodore Perry in the Land of the Shogun
resources.author: Rhoda Blumberg
resources.rationale: During this period in history, Japan had been isolated from the outside world for centuries. But when American Commodore Perry and his troops sailed to Japan with a letter from the President, everything began to change. This Newberry Honor book is an engaging way for students to explore global history, diplomacy, and cultural change through a well-told true story.

resources.title: Communicating with Grace and Virtue: Learning to Listen, Speak, Text, and Interact as a Christian
resources.author: Quentin Schultze
resources.rationale: An engaging and practical guide to help Christians interact effectively.

resources.title: Conscience: What It Is, How to Train It, and Loving Those Who Differ
resources.author: Andrew Naselli & J.D. Crowley
resources.rationale: This book is an exhortation to understand the basis of personal beliefs and relates how to extend grace when believers disagree.

resources.title: Cooking Class
resources.author: Deanna Cook
resources.rationale: This book teaches basic food preparation and cooking, using kid-friendly directions and recipes.

resources.title: Coolies
resources.author: Yin
resources.rationale: The experience of Chinese immigrants is captured in this book. During the mid-1800s thousands of Chinese workers came to America to build the Transcontinental Railroad. This book, which was inspired by actual events, is the story of two Chinese brothers who come to California to make a better life for themselves and their families back home. They find work helping to build the Transcontinental Railroad and find that life in America is much more difficult than they thought it would be.
resources.note: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Cross Country: Geography for Children
resources.author: Paul Hanna, Clyde Kohn
resources.rationale: Travel from Los Angeles to Washington, D.C. with Tom, Ruth, and their mother and father. As they encounter the different regions of the United States, the children learn about a variety of subjects, including basic geography concepts (landforms, waterbodies, and beginner map skills), as well as about such topics as local economies, climate, and regional plants and animals.
resources.note: Black-and-white version is available on Amazon for $16 ISBN: 978-1952118029.

resources.title: Cuentos que contaban nuestras abuelas (Tales Our Abuelitas Told)
resources.author: F. Isabel Campoy & Alma Flor Ada
resources.rationale: This book contains a retelling of twelve beloved stories that embody the lively spirit and the rich heritage of Latino people. This Spanish version is used in literature lessons.

resources.title: Culinary Reactions: The Everyday Chemistry of Cooking
resources.author: Simon Quellan Field
resources.rationale: The author explains the chemistry behind a variety of culinary methods.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: Curwen Music
resources.author: Mrs. Curwen & Kelli Rummel
resources.maySub:
local piano lessons. Curwen teacher preferred
➜ May also sub Hoffman Academy
resources.rationale: These are free video piano lessons for beginners that use the Curwen method.
resources.note: Preliminary and part of First Step lessons are available online; higher levels are incomplete.

resources.title: Dandelions
resources.author: Eve Bunting
resources.rationale: This longer-length picture book helps students understand westward expansion through the eyes of a pioneer family. The story shows what it was like to leave a familiar home, travel west, and settle on the Great Plains, highlighting the emotional and physical challenges families faced.
resources.note: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

resources.title: David Livingstone: African Explorer
resources.author: John Tiner
resources.rationale: Students will be intrigued by David Livingstone's (1813-1873) adventurous spirit, near-death experiences, as well as the flora and fauna of Africa. This book begins with David in his home country of Scotland and tells how he made his way to Africa. Our lesson plans provide map drills and mapping activities not only about Africa, but  also about how to create other kinds of maps based on one’s room or neighborhood.

resources.title: Delighting in the Trinity
resources.author: Michael Reeves
resources.rationale: In this lively book, we find an introduction to Christianity and the Christian life that is from start to finish rooted in our triune God―Father, Son, and Spirit.

resources.title: Denison Advanced Algebra & Trigonometry
resources.author: Denison
resources.rationale: Denison offers a complete high school math program with video lessons taught by a veteran teacher and homeschool dad for Pre-Algebra through Advanced Algebra/Trig. Pre-algebra only requires familiarity with the four basic functions (add, subtract, multiply, and divide). After Pre-Algebra, the course splits into two tracks for Algebra, Geometry, and Algebra 2: a track for students who don't have significant struggles but aren't enjoying math, and a Success track for students who struggle with math and need shorter lessons with more scaffolding.
resources.note: Choose this or Paul Foerster/Math Without Borders.

resources.title: Denison Algebra 1
resources.author: Denison
resources.rationale: Denison offers a complete high school math program with video lessons taught by a veteran teacher and homeschool dad for Pre-Algebra through Algebra 2. Pre-algebra only requires familiarity with the four basic functions (add, subtract, multiply, and divide). After Pre-Algebra, the course splits into two tracks for Algebra, Geometry, and Algebra 2: a track for students who don't have significant struggles but aren't enjoying math, and a Success track for students who struggle with math and need shorter lessons with more scaffolding.
resources.note:
Choose this or Elementary Algebra.
If following the Denison track, you only need to purchase this text for 8th-grade math. Use this in place of Elementary Algebra AND RightStart Geometry.

resources.title: Denison Algebra 2
resources.author: Denison

Pre-algebra only requires familiarity with the four basic functions (add, subtract, multiply, and divide). After Pre-Algebra, the course splits into two tracks for Algebra, Geometry, and Algebra 2: a track for students who don't have significant struggles but aren't enjoying math, and a Success track for students who struggle with math and need shorter lessons with more scaffolding.

If following the Denison track, you only need to purchase this text for 10th-grade math. Use this in place of Paul Foerster/Math Without Borders AND Harold Jacobs Geometry.

Pre-algebra only requires familiarity with the four basic functions (add, subtract, multiply, and divide). After Pre-Algebra, the course splits into two tracks for Algebra, Geometry, and Algebra 2: a track for students who don't have significant struggles but aren't enjoying math, and a Success track for students who struggle with math and need shorter lessons with more scaffolding.
resources.note:
Choose this or Harold Jacobs Geometry
If following the Denison track, you only need to purchase this text for 9th-grade math. Use this in place of Harold Jacobs Geometry AND Elementary Algebra.

Pre-algebra only requires familiarity with the four basic functions (add, subtract, multiply, and divide). After Pre-Algebra, the course splits into two tracks for Algebra, Geometry, and Algebra 2: a track for students who don't have significant struggles but aren't enjoying math, and a Success track for students who struggle with math and need shorter lessons with more scaffolding.
resources.note:
Choose this or Hands-On Equations
If following the Denison track, you only need to purchase this text for 7th-grade math. Use this in place of Hands-On Equations AND RightStart Geometry.

resources.title: Desert Giant
resources.author: Barbara Bash
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about a keystone species within the context of its community.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the saguaro cactus, a keystone species in the desert biome. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: Diez Deditos (Audio mp3)
resources.author: Jose-Luis Orozco
resources.maySub: favorite streaming service
resources.rationale: This is a collection of traditional songs in Spanish.
resources.note: Option 1 is a link to stream with Spotify, or Option 2 is a link to purchase the CD.

resources.title: Diez Deditos and Other Play Rhymes and Action Songs from Latin America
resources.author: José-Luis Orozco
resources.rationale: This is a collection of finger rhymes and action songs in Spanish.

resources.title: Dollars and Sense: A Kid's Guide to Using - Not Losing - Money
resources.author: Elaine Scott
resources.rationale: In this introduction to Economics, students will learn about the history of money, the banking system, and personal finance.

resources.title: Down Comes the Rain
resources.author: Franklyn M. Branley
resources.maySub: Water Dance by Thomas Locker if you own it
resources.rationale: This is a great book for students to learn about the water cycle. There are clear explanations and great illustrations.
resources.note: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Draw the Natural Wonders of Canada and Greenland: Geography Disguised as Fun
resources.author: Kristin Draeger
resources.rationale: After having mapped each of the Canadian provinces and territories - learning their boundaries, capitals, and major landmarks - students will then use this book for a more in-depth exploration of regional geography. Working from map templates, students will proceed to learn about and map the mountains, rivers, and lakes in Canada, and illustrate their maps with drawings of local flora and fauna.

resources.title: Drawing, Design, and Craft-Work
resources.author: Frederick J. Glass
resources.rationale: This is an excellent guide to art instruction, with suggestions for how to begin, materials to use, and the directions future work may take. This book is profusely illustrated with clear explanations.
resources.note: The free link is a different edition, and the lesson plans will have different page numbers. Download the file to view the free version. It will likely appear blank in the PDF reader.

resources.title: Early Christian Writings: The Apostolic Fathers edited
resources.author: Andrew Louth
resources.maySub: Free online sources.
resources.rationale: With the birth of Christianity came a lot of writings from a lot of different authors. In this collection, authors like Clement, Ignatius, Barnabas, and Polycarp contribute to the growth of Christianity and write documents against heresies, trying to answer questions of philosophy and questions about their God.

resources.title: Early Voices
resources.author: Mary Alice Downie
resources.rationale: This collection of writings by 29 different women presents a kaleidoscope image of Canada's history. The wide range of individual voices, backgrounds, and histories provided by these primary documents complement the other historical non-fiction and fiction read during the year.
resources.note: This book will be used over multiple years during high school

resources.title: Eat Well and Feel Great
resources.author: Tina Lond-Caulk
resources.rationale: Nutritionist Tina Lond-Caulk provides teens with an easy to use evidence based handbook filled with practical tips, recipes and illustrations that make the art of eating well an achievable goal.

resources.title: Economics in One Lesson
resources.author: Henry Hazlitt
resources.rationale: This classic guide to the basics of economic theory defends capitalism and the free market from economic myths that persist to this day.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Edgar Allan Poe: Complete Tales and Poems with Selected Essays
resources.author: Poe
resources.rationale: Classic words from time period author.

resources.title: Eiffel's Tower for Young People
resources.author: Rebecca Steffof
resources.rationale: Set in the late 1800s, the book follows engineer Gustave Eiffel and his team as they design and construct the tower for the 1889 Paris World’s Fair, a celebration of modern industry and innovation. The book places the Eiffel Tower in a global context, helping students see how world’s fairs, new technologies, and urban growth reflected broader historical shifts that were affecting many countries at the time. Students will also be introduced to other famous figures of that time including Annie Oakley, Dinah Salifou (king of Nalu people of Guinea), the Shah of Persia and the Van Gogh brothers. Architecture will also be integrated into the lesson plans.

resources.title: Elementary Algebra Student Book
resources.author: Harold Jacobs
resources.maySub: Algebra 1: from derekowens.com if you prefer an online course.
resources.rationale: This book uses inviting narrative and everyday problems to give an introduction to Algebraic concepts. Lots of practice problems are also included.
resources.note: Choose this or Denison. The "Playlist" link will take you to a YouTube playlist where a teacher goes through the lessons.

resources.title: Elementary Geography
resources.author: Charlotte Mason
resources.rationale: This is Charlotte Mason's classic introduction to Geography. We use selections from the book, which include many demonstrations, to help young children understand the concepts.

resources.title: Elementary Studies in Plant Life
resources.author: F.E. Fritsch & E. J. Salisbury
resources.rationale: This is a hands-on look at many aspects of how plants work in a classic, digital text.  The text provides many lab activities for the course.
resources.note: Annotations and updates to text included with lesson plans.

resources.title: Elijah of Buxton
resources.author: Christopher Paul Curtis
resources.rationale: This multi-award-winning novel brings to life the real town of Buxton, Ontario, a settlement of runaway slaves and free Black people that was a haven of safety and hope, especially in the years leading up to the Civil War. The story is told through the distinctive voice of Elijah, the first free-born child of Buxton, who is equal parts sensitive, naive, earnest, playful, and mischievous. When he is forced to discover the horrors of slavery first-hand, he demonstrates courage and maturity beyond his young age. Christopher Paul Curtis handles this difficult topic deftly, blending gentle humour and wisdom with a gripping plot of risk and danger.

resources.title: Emma's Poem: The Voice of the Statue of Liberty
resources.author: Linda Glaser
resources.rationale: This delightful picture book tells the fascinating story of the poem engraved on the Statue of Liberty.

resources.title: English Literature for Boys and Girls
resources.author: H.E. Marshall
resources.rationale: A delightful introduction to the writers of English literature whose works hold the greatest appeal for the youthful reader. The life and personality of each author is given in outline, with enough material quoted from key works to give an idea of the text.
resources.note: Selections used across multiple rotations.

resources.title: Erebus: The Story of a Ship
resources.author: Michael Palin
resources.rationale: Erebus is a book about exploration and mystery. Palin takes the student into two of the most harrowing exploration trips ever recorded, the last trip being unsuccessful and leading to a mystery that still puzzles modern-day minds. By reading Erebus, students will get an account of what is known about the first ever explorations into Antarctica, the most isolated continent in the world.

resources.title: Escape by Night: A Civil War Adventure
resources.author: Laurie Myers
resources.rationale: This historical fiction book does a great job of introducing slavery and the Civil War to younger students. The main character, Tommy, who lives in the South meets a wounded Union soldier, which brings the realities of opposing sides in the war into personal perspective. Tommy also questions the institution of slavery and his own views on justice and humanity. Another interesting aspect of the book is that the wounded soldier has a commonplace book and shares quotes with Tommy.

resources.title: Essay Voyage
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: The author's passion for writing comes through as he explains the ideas behind good essay writing.

resources.title: Essay Voyage Teacher Manual (select pages)
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.note: Used with publisher permission.

resources.title: ESV Concise Study Bible
resources.maySub: a translation of your choice but it is important to have one with study notes.
resources.rationale: A smaller-sized study Bible that is appropriate for this age. We highly suggest this Bible for the insightful study notes and accessibility for this age group. During lesson time, students will be asked to review the study notes and other explanatory material.
resources.note: This resource is for Grades 7-8 ONLY. Grades 9+ should use Cultural Backgrounds Study Bible.

resources.title: ESV Large Print Bible
resources.maySub: a translation of your choice.
resources.rationale: This large-print ESV Bible uses 12-point type for ease of reading

resources.title: Exploring the Frozen North
resources.author: Pierre Berton
resources.rationale: Pierre Berton's prose style and engaging narrative histories inspired generations of Canadians to treasure their nation's stories, and this compendium collects four of his books for younger readers in a single volume. The unknown coasts of the Arctic and the long-fabled Northwest Passage route to Asia loomed large in the nineteenth-century imagination, and this book introduces middle-grade readers to four important historical figures who shaped our modern knowledge of this region: William Edward Parry, Jane Franklin (Sir John Franklin's widow), Elisha Kent Kane, and Robert John McClure.

resources.title: Family Pictures/Cuadros de familia
resources.author: Carmen Lomas Garza
resources.rationale: The vibrant paintings and warm personal stories depict memories of the author growing up in a traditional Mexican-American family. This book is a great way to learn Spanish vocabulary during the picture study lessons.

resources.title: Fifty Steps in Sight-Singing
resources.author: Arthur Somervell
resources.rationale: Classic text to teach sight-singing.

resources.title: Five Months on the Missouri River: Paddling a Dugout Canoe
resources.author: Thomas Elpel
resources.rationale: You are on the river with Thomas and crew as they explore the Missouri from source to St. Louis in the reverse footsteps of Lewis and Clark noting what has changed and what remains the same.
resources.note: Please note that the spots on the pages are part of the book’s design and not a printing defect.

resources.title: Folding Techniques for Designers
resources.author: Paul Jackson
resources.rationale: Students will use the illustrations and directions in the books to do the assigned work.

resources.title: Forest Craft: A Child's Guide to Whittling in the Woodland
resources.author: Richard Irvine
resources.rationale: This is a book that can be turned to time and time again, through the different seasons and over many years. The skills learned can be treasured and passed down through generations in time-honored traditions.

resources.title: Frankenstein
resources.author: Mary Shelley
resources.rationale: A famous gothic novel first published in 1818 and then revised in 1831, Frankenstein influenced the development of science fiction. This gripping story was composed as a result of a writing challenge when the author was only 19, and it revolves around personhood, conscience, and the ethics of scientific ambition, themes which are especially important for students to wrestle with in today's age of AI.

resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This notebook contains markable copies of lesson readings, vocabulary lists, and lyrics for songs.
resources.note: This notebook is also available as part of hard-copy lessons in the print store.

resources.title: Frozen Land
resources.author: Jan Reynolds
resources.rationale: Kenalogak, a young girl, is part of the Inuit culture in Canada. How do people survive in such a cold climate? Watch as Kenalogak's family finds food and makes shelter and clothing for their survival.
resources.note: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

resources.title: General Biology, 2nd Edition
resources.author: Heather Ayala & Katie Rogstad
resources.rationale: In a conversational tone and with reverence, the authors of this general biology text clearly guide students from the molecules of biology to the complexities of the human body and evolution.

resources.title: Geometry Curriculum Pack
resources.author: Harold Jacobs
resources.maySub: Geometry: A Guided Inquiry by Chakerian (mathwithoutborders.com/geometry/)
resources.rationale: A solid and interesting HS geometry course for all.
resources.note: May add an online class from derekowens.com if desired.

resources.title: GeoPuzzles
resources.rationale: Kids will learn geography in a fun and engaging way, while discussing the different countries and how they are related.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book B
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: For the beginning reader, this book presents lowercase, capitals and numerals. With a short word and lively sentence on each page, this book engages the student.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book C
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: The simple transition to cursive italic is introduced. Basic writing practice themes include: modes of transportation (automobile through zeppelin), days of the week, months of the year, and sentences. Capitals practice: cities of the USA. Includes numerals.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book D
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: Writing practice for basic italic and cursive italic includes capitalization, vowel and consonant sounds, prefixes, suffixes, phonograms, homophones, tongue twisters, and six poem forms. Cursive capitals are introduced with historical development of each letter.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book E
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: While practicing cursive italic, students explore the many facets of the world of science — the animal kingdom, DNA, photosynthesis, minerals, continents, biomes, our solar system, and galaxy. Practice with capitals: cities of the world and history of letters.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book F
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: Figures of speech in the English language are used for cursive writing practice. Students will practice capital letters using the names of First Nation tribes and communities of North America. The exercises also explore the history of letters and numerals.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Getty-Dubay® Italic Handwriting Book G
resources.author: Handwriting Success
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Penny Gardner
resources.rationale: The final book in the Series contains basic and cursive writing practice to acquaint the student with the history of writing.  Part 3: edged pen writing.
resources.note: Choose this OR Italics by Penny Gardner. You do not need the Instruction Manual. Necessary instructions will be included in the lesson plans.

resources.title: Gladiola Garden
resources.author: Effie Lee Newsome
resources.rationale: This book by a lesser-known American poet contains beautiful nature poetry and illustrations for children.

resources.title: Globe
resources.maySub: any globe that shows both geographical features and place names
resources.rationale: We like that this globe is up to date and has raised landform features.

resources.title: God of All Things
resources.author: Andrew Wilson
resources.rationale: In God of All Things, pastor and author Andrew Wilson invites you to rediscover God through ordinary, everyday things. He explores the idea of a material world and presents a variety of created marvels that reveal the gospel in everyday life and fuel worship and joy in God.

resources.title: God's Bible Timeline: The Big Book of Biblical History
resources.author: Linda Finlayson
resources.rationale: Contains age-appropriate commentary on the eras of the Bible, along with detailed timelines, known dates, and a timeline poster. Lessons will refer to the timeline when reviewing stories and events.
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.

resources.title: God’s Big Picture: Tracing the Storyline of the Bible
resources.author: Vaughan Roberts
resources.rationale: Excellent overview of Bible history showing the outline of God's Kingdom plan from beginning to end.

resources.title: Going Home
resources.author: Marianne Berkes
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about animal migration.
resources.rationale: This lovely picture book introduces students to animal migration as a global phenomenon.  It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: Gold Mountain
resources.author: Betty G. Yee
resources.rationale: This historical fiction book introduces students to the Chinese immigrant experience in the 1800’s when thousands of Chinese workers came to the US to work on the Transcontinental Railroad. It is the story of a young Chinese girl who disguises herself as a boy and takes her deceased brother’s contract to work for the Central Pacific railroad. This book helps students get a better understanding of Chinese culture, the hardships and discrimination Chinese people faced, and the hard work it took to create the transcontinental railroad.

resources.title: Grade 4 Copywork & Dictation Book
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: Copywork and dictation passages taken from the books and poems from this year.
resources.note: Also included in the Grade 4 Bundles available with Alveary Printing store.
