resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: Copywork and dictation passages taken from the books and poems from this year.
resources.note: Also included in the Grade 8 Bundles available with Alveary Printing store.

resources.title: Grammar Island
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: This program brings story, delight, and ideas to the study of grammar. Grammar Island introduces, in a simple fashion, the full four-level grammar analysis of (1) parts of speech, (2) parts of the sentence, (3) phrases, and (4) clauses.

resources.title: Grammar Island Teacher Manual (select pages)
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.note: Used with publisher's permission.

resources.title: Grammar Town
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: Grammar Town builds on the foundation presented in Grammar Island and continues to bring delight and interest to the study of writing and grammar. It continues the full four-level grammar analysis of (1) parts of speech, (2) parts of the sentence, (3) phrases, and (4) clauses.

resources.title: Grammar Town Teacher Manual (select pages)
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.note: Used with publisher permission.

resources.title: Grammar Voyage
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: Building on grammar concepts introduced in Grammar Island and Grammar Town, this book continues to help students explore the intricacies of grammar.

resources.title: Grammar Voyage Teacher Manual (select pages)
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.note: Used with publisher permission.

resources.title: Grandma Gatewood Hikes the Appalachian Trail
resources.author: Jennifer Thermes
resources.rationale: This longer picture book tells the true story of a 67-year-old grandma who was the first woman to solo hike the Appalachian Trail. It is a great supplement to our geography spine and contains great maps to follow her journey through the trail.
resources.note: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons.

resources.title: Growing a Revolution: Bringing Our Soil Back to Life
resources.author: David Montgomery
resources.rationale: Discussion of modern agriculture, the nature of soil, and how to sustainably steward the earth.
resources.note:
For the Botany Elective in HS, this title is optional and allows the teacher to increase the literature component when appropriate.

For the Microbiology Elective in HS, choose this book OR Microbe Hunters.
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: Gullah Geechee Home Cooking: Recipes from the Matriarch of Edisto Island
resources.author: Emily Meggett
resources.maySub: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture
resources.note: Choose this OR Mexico in Your Kitchen, Classic Russian Cooking, Pomp and Sustenance, Celtic Folklore Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing

resources.title: Handbook of Nature Study
resources.author: Anna Comstock
resources.maySub: the full-color seven-volume set from Living Books Press
resources.rationale: A must-have for nature study and outdoor object lessons, this book is incorporated into teacher preparation for science lessons. Older children may read for themselves as they find things they are interested in on their walks.

resources.title: Hands-On Equations Deluxe Home Set
resources.author: Hands-On Equations
resources.rationale: Hands-On Equations is a very concrete introduction to abstract algebraic concepts.
resources.note:
Choose this or Denison.
The "Free" link will take you to a YouTube playlist where a master teacher goes through the first 22 lessons. 
➜ Manipulatives included.

resources.title: Hans Brinker or The Silver Skates
resources.author: Mary Mapes Dodge
resources.rationale: Mary Mapes Dodge was recognized as one of the leading authors of children's literature during the 19th century, and Hans Brinker is her most famous work. With themes of perseverance and selflessness, Hans Brinker's heartwarming tale of two siblings delights, instructs, and expands the imagination.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Heart and Soul: The Story of America and African Americans
resources.author: Kadir Nelson
resources.rationale: This book is written in the voice of an old African American grandmother taking a child on her knee to tell the stories of the black experience throughout American history. A couple of chapters are read each term. It will also be used the following year.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Heart of a Samurai
resources.author: Margi Preus
resources.rationale: Based on the true story of a Japanese boy who survived a shipwreck and was rescued by an American whaling ship, Heart of a Samurai functions as a transitional book between Grades 3-4. Starting in Grade 4, students study world history and read literature that increasingly coordinates with the time period. Set in both Japan and the US in the mid 19th century, this engaging narrative expands students' imaginations of the broader world which they will study in more detail in later years.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Henry IV Part 2
resources.author: William Shakespeare
resources.rationale: This history play is a tale about Prince Hal falling into his father's footsteps as king. Hal's life will no longer be the same when he feels the weight of the crown.

resources.title: Henry IV Part 2 (Audio recording)
resources.author: Arkangel
resources.maySub: Librivox recording

resources.title: Hiawatha and the Peacemaker
resources.author: Robbie Robertson
resources.rationale: This is the traditional story of how Hiawatha brought rival tribes together to defeat the evil Tadodaho and form the powerful Iroquois Nation in the 14th century.
resources.note: Some images may be scary for sensitive children. Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: High School Handbook
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This manual covers everything from transcripts to testing and from college to trade careers (plus a whole lot more). Like an Alveary lesson plan, this book is simply a tool that is only effective when backed by Charlotte Mason’s philosophy of education.

resources.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.
resources.author: Rand McNally
resources.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.

resources.title: History of Literature PDF
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: Curated selections of readings on literary developments, significant authors, and primary sources from the 19th century. Students use this PDF along with selected links and readings in Marshall's and Long's spines to deepen their relationship with 19th-century literature.

resources.title: Hoffman Academy
resources.author: Joseph Hoffman
resources.note: Free piano lessons are available online; premium membership is available ($20.00/mo.).

resources.title: Home Study Companion: Algebra 2/Trigonometry
resources.author: Math Without Borders
resources.rationale: Video lessons which accompany Foerster's Algebra 2/Trig

resources.title: Home Study Companion: Calculus
resources.author: Math Without Borders
resources.maySub: an online course with derekowens.com
resources.rationale: Video lessons to accompany Foerster's Calculus
resources.note: Begin Calculus when students finish Precalculus. Follow instructions to purchase textbook and materials in link.

resources.title: Home Study Companion: Physics
resources.author: Math Without Borders
resources.rationale: This living lecture series teaches the basic concepts of physics by guiding students to mathematically analyze moving objects in the world around them.

resources.title: Home Study Companion: Precalculus
resources.author: Math Without Borders
resources.maySub: an online course with derekowens.com
resources.rationale: Video lessons to accompany Foerster's Precalculus
resources.note: Begin Precalculus when students finish Algebra 2/Trig.  Follow instructions to purchase textbook and materials in link.

resources.title: How Different It Was: Canadians at the Time of Confederation
resources.author: Michael J. Goodspeed
resources.rationale: One of the key lessons of history is that the past was - to greater or lesser degrees - fundamentally different than the present, and this is shown clearly in Michael Goodspeed's book, How Different it Was: Canadians at the Time of Confederation. Goodspeed provides a multi-faceted account of the experiences of Canadians in the 1860s, exploring factors such as geographical differences, cultural practices, family dynamics, religious observances, scientific beliefs, leisure activities, habits, and attitudes, as well as the burgeoning nation of Canada's place in the wider nineteenth-century geopolitical arena. He draws heavily on primary source material, and the book is full of historical photographs and documents.
resources.note: E-book edition also widely available.

resources.title: HS Chem Intro
resources.author: Danielle Merritt-Sunseri
resources.note: Watch this introduction to glimpse our video companion lessons that accompany High School Chemistry.

resources.title: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China
resources.author: Janet & Geoff Benge
resources.rationale: Hudson Taylor (1832-1905), the founder of China Inland Mission, continues to inspire through the story of his love for the people of China and his trust in the Lord to provide financially for his ministry. Taylor faced persecution and loss in his own life in order to bring the gospel message to the “ripe harvest fields in China.”

resources.title: Hurlbut's Story of the Bible
resources.author: Jesse Lyman Hurlbut
resources.rationale: Contains stories from both the Old and New Testaments in a very engaging narrative; omits sections that are unsuitable for younger children. The author adheres to the Bible without embellishment, opinion, or doctrine, making this volume appropriate for any denomination.
resources.note: The free online option has different page numbers than the lesson plans, but the chapter numbers are the same.

resources.title: Hymns, Spirituals, & Folk Songs PDF
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: Selections are also included in Our Work packets for Grades 1-12. Audio lessons and other resources are available in lesson plans.

resources.title: I Am a Man: Chief Standing Bear's Journey for Justice
resources.author: Joe Starita
resources.rationale: This book is about Chief Standing Bear’s fight for justice as he navigates the loss of his land—taken by manipulation and coercion. Joe Starita uses primary sources to help the audience see many of the hardships that Chief Standing Bear went through — like the loss of his daughter and brother —and how others came to fight for him and his people. It grounds the reader in the time and experiences of another people group who were struggling to find their identity in a new nation.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: I Am Not a Ghost: The Canadian Pacific Railway
resources.author: David Bouchard
resources.rationale: This beautifully-illustrated book tells the story of Chinese railway workers whose contributions to the building of the Canadian Pacific Railway were essential, yet who faced racism, hardships, exploitation, and even death. At the heart of this story is friendship and hope, when one gravely ill Chinese man is saved and cared for by Amelia Douglas, a Métis woman who was the wife of the first governor of British Columbia, as well as a trained nurse and midwife.

resources.title: I Contain Multitudes
resources.author: Ed Yong
resources.rationale: Cutting-edge research on microbes.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: Illustrated Guide to Home Biology Experiments
resources.author: Robert & Barbara Thompson
resources.rationale: Used only for Microbiology experiments

resources.title: In the Days of Giants
resources.author: Abbie Farwell Brown
resources.rationale: The Greeks were not the only ones with a story tradition that influenced Western culture. These Norse stories have also been highly influential, and Brown has retold them beautifully.

resources.title: In the Shadow of the Alamo
resources.author: Sherry Garland
resources.rationale: By reading In the Shadow of the Alamo, students will be able to step into the shoes of a fifteen-year-old boy named Lorenzo Bonifacio who is plucked up by his government and has to fight in the Texas Revolution and the Alamo. Along the way, he experiences great friendships and great loss. Through reading this fictional book, students will get to learn about Mexico and its complicated history with the United States.

resources.title: Introducing the New Testament: A Historical, Literary, and Theological Survey
resources.author: Mark Allan Powell
resources.rationale: An overview of the NT for older students and educators providing book by book background and major themes to be used for years. The chapters can be read in any order allowing for use anywhere in the reading rotation. Powell presents various perspectives on interpretive issues to encourage engagement with ideas but does not attempt to resolve all the issues.  Artwork through the centuries adds a unique element that illustrates themes and demonstrates the influence of the NT over the centuries.
resources.note: Students will use this book for several years as they read through the NT.

resources.title: Introductory Chemistry: A Foundation
resources.author: Steven S. Zumdahl & Donald J. DeCoste
resources.rationale: Masters at inspiring and engaging students, the authors of this best-selling text combine problem-solving strategies, early coverage of chemical reactions, accessible explanations and visualizations, and an emphasis on everyday applications to facilitate understanding.
resources.note: Available in print or eBook with enhanced typesetting and audio. Previous edition is acceptable.

resources.title: Invincible Microbe
resources.author: Jim Murphy
resources.rationale: Murphy tells the story of an ancient and elusive microbe, weaving together ideas from science, history, and ethics. This author is a master storyteller, and we can't imagine a better way to continue our journey through natural history and into the very timely topic of microbiology and infectious disease.

resources.title: Italics: Beautiful Handwriting for Children
resources.author: Penny Gardner
resources.maySub: penmanship model of your choice or Getty-Dubay Handwriting Series
resources.rationale: We like this model because it is lovely, yet simple. Both the print and cursive models are in italic, making this a solid foundation for fine lettering later. Some may prefer a more traditional cursive model.
resources.note: Choose this OR Getty-Dubay Handwriting Series.

resources.title: Itse Selu: Cherokee Harvest Festival
resources.author: Daniel Pennington
resources.rationale: A captivating glimpse of Cherokee life in pre-Columbian North America. Join Little Wolf's family as they prepare to celebrate Itse Selu, the harvest festival. As night approaches the village, the magic of anticipation fills the air. The luscious feast, Grandfather's storytelling, and the sacred corn dance weave a magical tapestry of tradition.
resources.note: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

resources.title: James
resources.author: Percival Everett
resources.rationale: Recently published and already widely acclaimed, James retells Twain's Adventures of Huckleberry Finn from Jim's point of view. Funny and intense by turns and always insightful, Everett immerses students in 19th-century America. Alveary lessons pair selections of Huckleberry Finn alongside James to enrich students' understanding of the texts and times.
resources.note:
Only recommended for mature 11-12th grade students. Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

Choose James OR Jane Eyre. Students more interested in American Lit should consider James. Those more interested in British Lit should consider Jane Eyre.

resources.title: Jane Eyre
resources.author: Charlotte Brontë, edited by Karen Swallow Prior
resources.rationale: A coming of age story narrated in first person and set in 19th century England. Students experience the world through Jane's eyes and wrestle along with her when she faces various events and quandaries.
resources.note:
This edition includes the full text along with reflection questions and an introduction. Since the lesson plans refer to this additional content, students should use this edition if possible.  Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

Choose Jane Eyre OR James. Students more interested in American Lit should consider James. Those more interested in British Lit should consider Jane Eyre.

resources.title: Jeremy's War 1812
resources.author: John Ibbitson
resources.rationale: A gripping account of the War of 1812 told from the perspective of Jeremy Fields, a fifteen-year-old orphaned boy who joins a regiment of British soldiers and eventually becomes the personal servant of Sir Isaac Brock. Historical details blend smoothly with a fictional story to create a detailed recreation of this definitive period in Canada's pre-Confederation history.

resources.title: Joe and the Wreck of the Tribune
resources.author: Jacqueline Halsey
resources.rationale: At the turn of the nineteenth century, a thirteen-year old boy risked his life to save drowning sailors from the wreck of the H.M.S. Tribune, off the coast of Nova Scotia. This historical fiction novel imagines a backstory for the boy only known to history as "Joe Cracker," and it paints a detailed picture of what life was like in a small fishing village around 1800.

resources.title: Jump Back, Paul: The Life and Poems of Paul Laurence Dunbar
resources.author: Sally Derby
resources.rationale: An engaging narrative telling about the life and times of Paul Laurence Dunbar, one of the most widely acclaimed American poets at the end of the 19th century. Dunbar's poetry is woven throughout the narrative so students can understand its context and significance.

resources.title: Lars Romam Odit: A Latin Novella
resources.author: Andrew Olimpi
resources.rationale: Written with the novice-level reader of Latin in mind—low unique word count, a full glossary, and many illustrations--Lars Romam Odit is an ideal first encounter in an exciting period of Roman history.
resources.note: Only needed if using Alveary Online course.

resources.title: Laura Secord's Brave Walk
resources.author: Connie Brummel Crook
resources.rationale: Laura Secord's Brave Walk introduces young readers to the War of 1812 through the courage of one Canadian, who embarked on a long journey through a forest at night to deliver a message to the British troops. The engaging narrative and illustrations bring this episode of Canadian history to life for young readers.

resources.title: Les plus belles fables de La Fontaine
resources.author: Jean de La Fontaine

resources.title: Life on Surtsey: Iceland's Upstart Island
resources.author: Loree Griffin Burns
resources.rationale: In 1963, a volcano erupted and created a new island off the coast of Iceland. This book tells the story of the scientists who are watching and recording life unfold through primary succession.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: Like a Bird: The Art of the American Slave Song
resources.author: Cynthia Grady
resources.rationale: This is a collection of African American spirituals, along with their stories and beautiful illustrations.
resources.note: 1-2 songs from this book are chosen most years.

resources.title: Lincoln: A Photobiography
resources.author: Russell Freedman
resources.rationale: This book follows the events leading up to, during, and after the Civil War through the life of Abraham Lincoln.  As students read about Lincoln’s personal and public struggles, students will learn about the character and leadership of this man during one of the most tumultuous times of our nation’s history. This is the first war during which photography was used to show the people back home what war was really like, so the images capture not only the events, but also the new technology of the age.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Linnea in Monet's Garden
resources.author: Christina Björk
resources.rationale: This beautifully illustrated children’s book introduces readers to art, history, travel, and the life of the famous Impressionist painter Claude Monet through the eyes of a curious young girl. The watercolor paintings and reproductions of Monet’s work provide a great way for students to learn French vocabulary during the picture study lessons.

resources.title: Little Women
resources.author: Louisa May Alcott
resources.rationale: A beloved piece of 19th century literature, this story follows four sisters as they grow and learn to live full and happy lives. The story is loosely set during the American Civil War and has remained popular since it was first published.
resources.note:
Grades 7-8: Choose this OR Sir Gibbie.
Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Luke for Everyone
resources.author: N.T. Wright
resources.rationale: Wright provides a great first commentary experience for high school students.  The familiar tone and approach is very accessible and helpful as a companion read to the Gospel of Luke.

resources.title: Making of Canada: An Epic History in Twenty Extraordinary Lives
resources.author: Greg Koabel
resources.rationale: In this engaging book, Greg Koabel challenges the notion that pre-Confederation Canada unfolded in a linear fashion and instead suggests that nation-building involved greater "regional, national, and human diversity" than is typically acknowledged. To illustrate this argument, Koabel introduces his readers to twenty episodes significant to Canada's history but whose major players have been largely forgotten. These biographical vignettes give readers a more personal - and memorable - connection to Canadian history.
resources.note: This book will be used over two years in high school (Rotation 2 and Rotation 3). In 2026-2027, students will read the second half of the book. Interested students may wish to read the first half over the summer, but it is not necessary since all chapters are self-contained.

resources.title: Making Your Own Maps
resources.author: Susan Hansen
resources.rationale: This book provides great examples of different kinds of maps that students will be creating in their geography lessons.
resources.note: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons.

resources.title: Mama Built a Little Nest
resources.author: Jennifer Ward
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about birds and their different types of nests.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the variety of ways that birds build homes for their offspring. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: Maple Moon
resources.author: Connie Brummel Crook
resources.rationale: In Maple Moon, Connie Brummel Crook borrows from traditional folk tales to create an enchanting fictional account of how maple syrup might have been discovered. The protagonist, a young Mississauga boy named Rides the Wind, learns of the sweet water because his physical limitations prevent him from joining a hunt; yet, it is this discovery that helps his community survive the winter. Illustrated by beautiful paintings, this book teaches children that strength can come in many forms.

resources.title: Mapping the World with Art
resources.author: Ellen Johnston McHenry
resources.rationale: This book is a blend of history, geography, and art. The text is about the history of cartography, from the earliest maps that were drawn on clay by the people of Mesopotamia to the exploration of the "new world."  This book also includes map drawing lessons that take students step by step through the process of drawing maps of all the countries mentioned in the text.
resources.note: We highly recommend purchasing the videos that guide students through the map drawings. The option linked includes a digital version of the book along with the video course, but you can also purchase the videos in DVD or USB format if you prefer a physical copy of the book. The discount code is valid for all products on the author's site.

resources.title: Master of Field Arts
resources.author: Roseann Hanson
resources.rationale: Welcome to the next level for nature journalers and field sketchers—a deep dive into becoming a dedicated master naturalist and field artist.

resources.title: Mastering Hand Building
resources.author: Sunshine Cobb
resources.rationale: From pinch pots to coiled boxes to soft slab tableware, mastering hand building is a lifelong pursuit. This book covers all the foundational skills, with lessons for constructing both simple and complex forms from clay.

resources.title: Math Card Games Kit
resources.author: RightStart
resources.maySub: any games that allow students to practice math concepts and skills.
resources.rationale:
Kit is recommended for those who do not use RightStart Math; kit is included in the RS2 Math Set. 
➜  Additional recommended game titles will be listed with the Lesson Plans for each level of mathematics.

resources.title: Me on the Map
resources.author: Joan Sweeney
resources.rationale: This book explains the many ways in which we can describe where we are located. Beginning in a little girl's room, we gradually zoom out to her house, her street, her town, her state, her country, and her planet.

resources.title: Mexico in Your Kitchen
resources.author: Mely Martínez
resources.maySub: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture
resources.note: Choose this OR Gullah Geechee Home Cooking, Classic Russian Cooking, Pomp and Sustenance, Celtic Folklore Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing

resources.title: Michael Faraday's The Chemical History of a Candle
resources.author: Bill Hammack & Don DeCoste
resources.rationale: This book introduces modern readers to Michael Faraday’s great nineteenth-century lectures on "The Chemical History of a Candle." Students will be watching the lectures on video, so this text is for teachers or for students who would like to follow along using the text.

resources.title: Micro Life
resources.author: DK/Smithsonian Institution
resources.rationale: An amazing close-up look at all aspects of micro life!
resources.note: Choose this book OR Microbe Hunters

resources.title: Microbe Hunters
resources.author: Paul De Kruif
resources.rationale: Fascinating biographies of famous scientists.
resources.note:
Choose this book OR A Planet of Viruses, Trees Up Close, & Growing a Revolution.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: Mini-Farming: Self-Sufficiency on 1/4 Acre
resources.author: Brett Markham
resources.rationale: This resource is for the more seasoned gardener who is interested in self-sufficiency and conservation.

resources.title: Mission to Pluto: The First Visit to an Ice Dwarf and the Kuiper Belt
resources.author: Mary Kay Carson
resources.rationale: Students learn about the discovery of Pluto and join the scientists who are learning about our solar system. How do we learn about very big things and those that are very far away? We find out on a Mission to Pluto!
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: Monarch and Milkweed
resources.author: Helen Frost
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about an insect within the context of its plant community.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to an insect and its relationship with the environment. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: Moonstick: The Seasons of the Sioux
resources.author: Eve Bunting
resources.rationale: A young boy comes of age under the thirteen moons of the Sioux year. With each notch in his father's moon-counting stick, the boy marvels at the world around him, observing the sometimes subtle, sometimes remarkable, changes in the seasons and in his own tribe's way of living.
resources.note: Used briefly. You may wish to check out from the library.

resources.title: Mussorgsky's Pictures at an Exhibition
resources.author: Anna Harwell Celenza
resources.rationale: This book tells the sad but touching story behind Mussorgysky's famous composition to honor his friend's paintings.
resources.note: For Composer Study Term 3, choose either Blind Tom OR Modest Mussorgsky's "Pictures at an Exhibition" to study for the first part of the term. This book is optional and is not required to complete the lessons.

resources.title: My Canada: An Illustrated Atlas
resources.author: Katherine Dearlove
resources.rationale: A visually appealing atlas for young learners, this fun book makes a good companion to studying the provinces in The Kids Book of Canada.

resources.title: Napoleon
resources.author: David A. Bell
resources.rationale: Very readable biography of this important historical figure.

resources.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
resources.author: Frederick Douglass
resources.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
resources.note: GRADES 9+ should choose this OR Up From Slavery and Souls of Black Folk.

resources.title: Nature All Around: Plants
resources.author: Pamela Hickman
resources.rationale: We have chosen this lovely companion to our picture book selection for our 5th graders' introduction to botany. This title uses simple language, introduces new terms, and incorporates artwork that is both beautiful and useful for students who are ready to get to know the world of plants more closely!

resources.title: Nature Journaling for a Wild Life
resources.author: Roseann Beggy Hanson
resources.rationale: Explore the enriching world of nature journaling in an eight-week, self-guided “workshop in a book” from explorer, naturalist, and artist Roseann Hanson.

resources.title: Next Time You See a Cloud
resources.author: Emily Morgan
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book calls attention to physical features in the larger ecosystem: weather changes and the movement of water. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: NIV Cultural Backgrounds Study Bible: Bringing to Life the Ancient World of Scripture
resources.author: Zondervan
resources.maySub: a translation of your choice.
resources.rationale: Contains unabridged Scripture along with important archaeological, historical, and cultural background.
resources.note: This resource is for Grades 9+ ONLY. Grades 7-8 should use the ESV Concise Study Bible.

resources.title: North American Cambridge Latin Unit 1 5th Ed. Student Book
resources.author: Cambridge School Classics Project
resources.rationale: This program continues presenting narrative stories in Latin, grammar exercises, vocabulary, and historical notes in an engaging way.

resources.title: North American Cambridge Latin Unit 2 5th Ed. Student Book
resources.author: Cambridge School Classics Project
resources.rationale: Contains comic strips and narrative stories in Latin, grammar exercises, vocabulary, and historical notes.

resources.title: North American Cambridge Latin Unit 3 5th Ed.
resources.rationale: This is the student book and contains comic strips and narrative stories in Latin, grammar exercises, vocabulary, and historical notes.
resources.note: This edition comes with 1-year access to Elevate/Cambridge Go.

resources.title: Number Stories of Long Ago
resources.author: David Smith
resources.maySub: another edition, but page numbers will be different
resources.rationale: This is the story of how mathematics developed out of the early need for humans to have a language for "how many" and evolved into the robust system of arithmetic we know today.

resources.title: Oaxaca Journal
resources.author: Oliver Sacks
resources.rationale: A master writer takes you on a trip to discover ferns in southern Mexico in this work of botany literature.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: Old Testament Today
resources.author: John H. Walton & Andrew E. Hill
resources.rationale: It is so important for students to understand the context in which the Bible was written and what it meant to the original audience. However, it is also important to understand how the OT writings are relevant to us today. The authors do a wonderful job communicating all these ideas. Lesson plans will guide you in navigating this rich text.
resources.note: Students will use this book for several years as they read through the OT.

resources.title: On the Trapline
resources.author: David A. Robertson
resources.rationale: This is an award-winning picture book, which tells the gentle story of a boy accompanying his grandfather on a trip back to his childhood home where he and his family lived off the land.

resources.title: Our Canadian Flag
resources.author: Maxine Trottier
resources.rationale: This book offers a patriotic look at the Canadian flag by exploring its history and significance for the whole country. Each page is beautifully illustrated and addresses a different theme by presenting both a narrative description and specific facts and examples that can provide interesting avenues for further learning.

resources.title: Our Hymns, Our Heritage
resources.author: David & Barbara Leeman
resources.rationale: This student hymnal contains multi-denominational hymns organized by the church year, with brief histories of the music and lyrics, bios of the writers, related Scripture, and other ideas to meditate upon.

resources.title: Our States Have Crazy Shapes
resources.author: Lynn Garthwaite
resources.rationale: Short chapter vignettes will explain how states ultimately received their shapes.  Each state has a story and reason for having the border lines they do.  Students will sketch their own maps based on what they learn.

resources.title: Our Work: Grade 3
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

resources.title: Our Work: Grades 1-2
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

resources.title: Our Work: Grades 4-6
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

resources.title: Ourselves Book 2: Self-Direction
resources.author: Charlotte Mason
resources.maySub: any edition.
resources.rationale: Mason emphasized that children are persons; this is the volume in which she defines that term. What characteristics do we, as Image-Bearers, have in common? What kinds of pitfalls should we anticipate? These are some of the profound questions Mason deals with in this, her only volume written directly to students.

resources.title: Ourselves: Book 1
resources.author: Charlotte Mason
resources.maySub: any edition.
resources.rationale: Mason emphasized that children are persons; this is the volume in which she defines that term. What characteristics do we, as Image-Bearers, have in common? What kinds of pitfalls should we anticipate? These are some of the profound questions Mason deals with in this, her only volume written directly to students.

resources.title: Over and Under the Pond
resources.author: Kate Messner
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book about pond habitats as preferred by the teacher.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes us on a trip to the pond alongside mother and son. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: Over the River and Through the Wood: An Anthology of Nineteenth-Century American Children's Poetry
resources.author: Karen L. Kilcup (Editor) & Angela Sorby (Editor)
resources.maySub: Public domain links
resources.rationale: A beautiful collection of 19th century children's poetry for all ages.
resources.note: Grades 5-6 can substitute A Poem for Every Day of the Year edited by Allie Esiri if desired.

resources.title: Pacific Coast Tree Finder
resources.author: Tom Watts
resources.rationale: Regional guide
resources.note: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

resources.title: Paper Folding Video Lessons
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: Teaches basic paper folding techniques and helps young students gain practice in fine motor skills.

resources.title: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades
resources.author: Ednah Rich
resources.rationale: This book begins with very simple models; difficulty increases gradually.
resources.note: Be sure to purchase the Yesterday's Classics edition, as other editions have issues with formatting and readability. Video tutorials are provided with lesson plans.

resources.title: Paragraph Town
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: This program brings story, delight, and ideas to the study of grammar. Paragraph Town gives children an entertaining and comprehensive introduction to the paragraph. With a narrative background, students explore the importance of organizing sentences into clear, orderly paragraphs with dialogue, description, comparison, punctuation, and grammar, as well as a look at the significance of the sounds of language. Structured exercises and assignments allow students to practice integrating these ideas into their writing.

resources.title: Periodic Table of Elements 1000 Piece Puzzle
Phineas Gage
resources.author: John Fleischman
resources.rationale: In this classic story, readers hear the story of a man who suffers an horrific railroad accident.  The resulting brain injury forever changes his life and produces one the most fascinating case studies that neuroscience has ever seen. Note that the author associates social ability and success with human value. Discuss with students as appropriate.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: Pilgrim's Progress: Updated, Modern English
resources.author: John Bunyan
resources.maySub: original text.
resources.rationale: Mason's students read the original text, and some Alveary members choose to do the same. However, we find this retelling robust and beautiful, without the archaic vocabulary of the original, making the ideas more accessible to young children.
resources.note: Grade 3: Students should read EITHER Pilgrim's Progress OR A Wonder Book.

resources.title: Plate Tectonics
resources.author: Fiona Young-Brown
resources.rationale: This text explores the field of plate tectonics from its early history to the current era.  Students will consider how such theories explain the natural phenomena that have contributed to the Earth's development, as well as events such as volcanoes and earthquakes.

resources.title: Playlist for Afternoon Listening and Selected Lessons
resources.author: Alveary

resources.title: Poetry
resources.author: Various
resources.maySub: an anthology of 19th century poetry such as the one used in younger grades.
resources.rationale: Lesson plans draw in additional poetry from the 19th century to broaden the student's encounter with poetry from this time period.

resources.title: Poetry Anthology
resources.author: Various
resources.rationale: Students use a poetry anthology of their choice (in print or online) for the exercises accompanying readings in The Word Made Fresh. Suggestions are listed in Extra Helpings for the course, but any anthology with a wide variety of poems and poets is sufficient.

resources.title: Pomp and Sustenance
resources.author: Mary Taylor Simeti
resources.maySub: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture
resources.note: Choose this OR Mexico in Your Kitchen, Classic Russian Cooking, Gullah Geechee Home Cooking, Celtic Folklore Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing

resources.title: Pottery for Beginners
resources.author: Ruth Baldwin
resources.rationale: Unleash your inner creativity and master the art of pottery with this complete craft guide to the wonders of ceramics.

resources.title: Practice Island
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: Practice Island is a workbook with practice sentences that is used in conjunction with Grammar Island.

resources.title: Practice Voyage Teacher Manual (select pages)

resources.note: Used with publisher permission.

resources.title: Primary French Course Part 2
resources.author: Otto Siepmann
resources.rationale: This book teaches vocabulary and grammar through reading, writing, and spoken exercises.

resources.title: Primary French Course Part I
resources.author: Otto Siepmann
resources.rationale: This book teaches vocabulary and grammar through reading, writing, and spoken exercises.

resources.title: Prisoners of Geography: Ten Maps That Explain Everything About the World
resources.author: Tim Marshall
resources.rationale: A fascinating look at several regions of the world and how geography, history, and politics are all interconnected.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Reading American Horizons
resources.author: Various Authors
resources.rationale: From sources like Andrew Jackson’s “Veto of the Bank of the United States" to “the Declaration of Sentiments,” students will get their hands on written and visual documents that will help them see firsthand what people in the 1800s were encountering on a day-to-day basis. Students will develop their historical thinking skills by using these primary sources.

resources.title: Reading Lessons: Level 1 (Revised 2025)
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This program features 15-minute lessons focusing on phonics and high-frequency word recognition and utilizing classic stories and poems from the Free and Treadwell Readers. The lessons include spelling, copywork, dictation, and some grammar. This program reflects Mason's principles as well as recent research, especially the importance of phonological awareness and phonics.
resources.note: Will also be available in Grade 1 Bundle with Alveary printing store after June 1st.

resources.title: Reading Lessons: Level 2 (Revised 2025)
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This program features 15-minute lessons focusing on phonics and high-frequency word recognition and utilizing classic stories and poems from the Free and Treadwell Readers. The lessons include spelling, copywork, dictation, and some grammar. This program reflects Mason's principles as well as recent research, especially the importance of phonological awareness and phonics.
resources.note: Will also be available in Grade 1 Bundle with Alveary printing store after June 1st.

resources.title: Reading Lessons: Level 3
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This program features 15-minute lessons focusing on phonics and high-frequency word recognition and utilizing classic stories and poems from the Free and Treadwell Readers. The lessons include spelling, copywork, dictation, and some grammar. This program reflects Mason's principles as well as recent research, especially the importance of phonological awareness and phonics.
resources.note: Will also be available in Grade 3 Bundle with Alveary printing store after June 1st.

resources.title: Reading PDF
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: Students will learn about important historical documents developed between 1800-1900. Following the founding of the country, the framework of the nation begins to take shape. The Missouri Compromise sets the stage for the Civil War as new states join the nation; the federal government experiences internal conflict as the Executive Branch disregards the Judicial Branch over the sovereignty of tribal nations; and the Reconstruction Amendments are added to the Constitution.  A "Reading PDF" will be included in the lesson plans that provides accessible analysis integrated with relevant excerpts of Supreme Court decisions, legislative acts, and other texts as appropriate.

resources.title: Reading the Bible for a Change
resources.author: Ray Lubeck
resources.rationale: Ray Lubeck’s Reading the Bible for a Change teaches readers how to approach the bible and how to relate it to our lives. Through careful study, a student will learn how to approach the various genres of the Old and New Testaments, as well as how to respond and apply them to one’s life.

resources.title: Reading-Literature: First Reader
resources.author: Free and Treadwell
resources.rationale: This is a great collection of folk tales and poems for a beginning reader. This book series lends itself well to Mason's approach to teaching children to read because of the repetitive pattern of the stories and the decodable text. Use with our Reading Lessons: Level 2.

resources.title: Reading-Literature: Second Reader
resources.author: Free and Treadwell
resources.rationale: This is a great collection of folk tales and poems for a beginning reader. This book series lends itself well to Mason's approach to teaching children to read because of the repetitive pattern of the stories and the decodable text. Use with our Reading Lessons: Level 3.

resources.title: Reading-Literature: The Primer
resources.author: Free and Treadwell
resources.rationale: This is a great collection of folk tales and poems for a beginning reader. This book series lends itself well to Mason's approach to teaching children to read because of the repetitive pattern of the stories and the decodable text. Use with our Reading Lessons: Level 1.

resources.title: Red Wolf
resources.author: Jennifer Dance
resources.rationale: A harrowing and heartbreaking story of the impact of the Indian Act of 1876 on Indigenous families and communities through one child's experience of the Indian Residential School system. This novel is beautifully written and rich in historical detail, presenting an honest introduction to a dark chapter of Canada’s history.

resources.title: Redeeming Vision
resources.author: Elissa Weichbrodt
resources.rationale: Educators of all levels and high school students take the next step in deepening their relationship with art by contemplating a theology of art for viewers which impacts how we see as well as how we are and how we love.  Practice in taking what has been learned in previous years along with new aspects of art elements and themes is guided by Dr. Weichbrodt.

resources.title: Redemption: The Church in Ancient Times
resources.author: Luke H. Davis
resources.rationale: Students will learn about Christians from this time period whose faith led many of them to martyrdom.  Beginning with Peter at Pentecost, the author tells about saints such as Ignatius, Cyprian and Athanasius who defended the truths of Christianity against heresy. Instead of just giving us facts about the different people, the author tells a story about a time in their life that helps the reader understand who they are and why their story is recorded in history. This volume is a great way for students to see how these important church fathers fit into the stream of history. These were the life and times they were living— when the persecution from the Roman Empire was prevalent.

resources.title: Redwoods
resources.author: Jason Chin
resources.rationale: This book about the redwood trees interweaves a story about a boy traveling to the forest and includes facts about these massive trees.
resources.note: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Renewal: The Church That Expands Outward
resources.author: Luke H. Davis
resources.rationale: Students will read the last half of the book this year as they continue to see how the church expanded globally through preaching, mission work, and social work in the name of Christ. The author tells the story of a pivotal event in the life of faithful Christians including William Wilberforce, Sojourner Truth, Hudson Taylor and more. Lesson plans will also include necessary background information, links, and other pertinent PDFs and articles.
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.

resources.title: RightStart Mathematics Level A 2nd Ed. Book Bundle

RightStart is based on discovery using a wide range of manipulatives. It is an up-to-date, spiraling program that uses methods that are heavily supported by current research.

resources.note:

resources.rationale: Local geology guides.

resources.rationale: Regional guide
resources.note: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

resources.author: Sharon Lovejoy
resources.rationale: This book includes lots of ideas for beginner gardeners that will be a joy to both you and your students.

resources.title: Rubicon
resources.author: Tom Holland
resources.rationale: Historical account of Rome's transition from Republic to Empire.
resources.note: Sensitive Content. Optional read for interested students. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Russian Fairy Tales
resources.author: Alexander Afanasyev
resources.rationale: Pushkin, Afanasyev, and many others wrote and collected Russian folk tales in the 19th century. Students read a few tales in the lesson plans and may enjoy the others on their own time.
resources.note: Although the free and hard copy editions are not identical, they are from the same translator with nearly identical language. The tales scheduled in the lesson plans are included in both texts and are only scheduled for a few weeks.

resources.title: Sacajawea
resources.author: Joseph Bruchac
resources.rationale: By blending careful historical research with storytelling, Bruchac presents the Lewis and Clark expedition through an Indigenous perspective. This novel helps students see Sacajawea as a complex, intelligent young woman whose language skills, cultural knowledge, and resilience were essential to the expedition’s survival.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
resources.author: Samin Nosrat
resources.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: Schaum's Outlines: College Physics
resources.author: Eugene Hecht, PhD
resources.rationale: This book is listed as a resource/supplement to the Math with Borders Physics video course.

resources.title: Science: Grade 4 Lab Book
resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 4, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The teacher's primary role is teaching students to follow directions.
resources.note:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 4 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book.  It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term. 

NOTE: Also included in Grade 4 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 7, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The lab book builds upon those from Form 2 (Grades 4-6) but also supports those new to the program in Form 3 (Grades 7-8). It has been designed for a gentler transition to high school science, as well.
resources.note:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 7 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.  

NOTE: Also included in Grade 7 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

resources.author: Alveary
resources.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 8, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The lab book is designed to provide a foundation for the high school disciplines.
resources.note:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 8 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.  

NOTE: Also included in Grade 8 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

resources.title: SeaMan: The Dog Who Explored The West With Lewis & Clark
resources.author: Gail Langer Karwoski
resources.rationale: The exciting adventures of Lewis and Clark are captured in this engaging book. This fictional account is full of accurate details drawn from Lewis’s own diary entries. This book provides students an interesting and accessible way to understand the challenges, dangers, and daily life of the Lewis and Clark expedition.

resources.title: Sentence Island
resources.author: Michael Clay Thompson
resources.rationale: This program brings story, delight, and ideas to the study of grammar. In this book, students focus on the science and art of forming a good sentence and learn the rules and concepts that are essential for formal writing.

resources.title: Sewing School
resources.author: Andria Lisle and Amie Petronis Plumley
resources.rationale: This book teaches basic stitches and techniques and includes lots of simple sewing projects for the beginner.

resources.title: Shanleya's Quest Card Game
resources.author: Thomas Elpel
resources.rationale: Botany in a Day author, Thomas Elpel, produced this deck of cards to help students begin to notice the patterns that are used to characterize common plant families.
resources.note:
For Grade 5, this resource is not required but is highly recommended for optional activities included in the lesson plans.
For the Botany Elective in HS, this resource is REQUIRED.

resources.title: Short Stories & Folktales
resources.author: Various
resources.rationale: Lesson plans curate and include additional folk tales and shorter writings from 19th century authors such as Tolstoy and George Macdonald.

resources.title: Short stories and other writings
resources.author: Various
resources.rationale: Lesson plans curate a selection of writings from a variety of 19th century authors such as Pushkin, O.Henry, Tagore, and others to give students exposure to a broader range of genres, authors, and ideas.

resources.title: Silas Marner
resources.author: George Eliot (Mary Ann Evans)
resources.rationale: Students spend two terms lingering with this nineteenth-century novel which depicts British village life while stretching the reader's moral imagination. Composition lessons build on these lessons by asking students to analyze characters, compare characters’ development during the two time periods of the novel, and use the events of the novel to discuss ethical questions.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Simply Clean: The Proven Method for Keeping Your Home Organized, Clean, and Beautiful in Just 10 Minutes a Day
resources.author: Becky Rapinchuk
resources.rationale: This is a resource for parents who would like some help developing a cleaning rhythm in their homes. It includes step-by-step instructions on how to clean and perform other household tasks.

resources.title: Sir Gibbie
resources.author: George MacDonald
resources.rationale: Although less well known than some of his friends, George Macdonald cast an outsized influence on his contemporaries and many 20th century writers who followed him, including C.S. Lewis and J.R.R. Tolkein. Macdonald's genius for portraying goodness and inspiring readers is on full display in this rags-to-riches tale of a mute boy from the Scotch Highlands.
resources.note: Choose this OR Little Women.

resources.title: Sloyd
resources.author: Everett Schwartz
resources.rationale: This book contains models of geometric solids that support the study of geometry.
resources.note: Video tutorials for certain models provided with lesson plans. Students work independently on others.

resources.title: Song in a Rainstorm: The Story of Musical Prodigy Thomas "Blind Tom" Wiggins
resources.author: Glenda Armand
resources.rationale: "Had he been born in our time," the author writes, "he might have been called an autistic musical savant." Students will enjoy this picture book biography of an incredible American performer and composer during the last half of the 19th century.
resources.note: For Composer Study Term 3, choose either Blind Tom OR Modest Mussorgsky's "Pictures at an Exhibition" to study for the first part of the term. Blind Tom is recommended for younger students.

resources.title: Sophie's Rebellion
resources.author: Beverley Boissery
resources.rationale: Sophie's Rebellion is a fictional account of one American girl's experience of the 1837-1838 Rebellions, as she is held prisoner alongside her British travelling companions and befriends a young rebel named Luc. Through Sophie's eyes, the reader is able to see multiple perspectives of this conflict and is introduced to a number of notable historical figures. A trained historian, Beverley Boissery creates an authentically detailed account of Upper and Lower Canada in the mid-nineteenth century.

resources.title: South American Jungle Tales
resources.author: Horacio Quiroga
resources.rationale: A Uruguayan author and playwright who lived at the end of the 19th century, Quiroga is sometimes compared to Rudyard Kipling and Edgar Allen Poe.
resources.note: Students only read a tale or two from this collection, so the free version is recommended.

resources.title: Spurgeon: A Biography
resources.author: Arnold Dallimore
resources.rationale: This biography provides an introduction to Charles Spurgeon (1834-1892), an influential preacher who started the Metropolitan Tabernacle in London, England. Spurgeon is often called “The Prince of Preachers,” and this biography shows his life of faith, his personal struggles with depression, his humor, and his love for his family as he strived to preach the gospel so that all might hear.

resources.title: Steering the Craft: A Twenty-First Century Guide to Sailing the Sea of Story
resources.author: Ursula Le Guin
resources.rationale: A master writer gives an interesting overview of narrative techniques and other writing principles to help students write effectively and pay attention more closely to the books they are reading.

resources.title: Stories From the History of Rome
resources.author: Emily Beesly
resources.rationale: This is a narrative introduction to the stories students will read later in Plutarch's Lives.
resources.note: Contains some violence that may be disturbing for sensitive children.
