resources.author: Julia Diggins
resources.maySub: previous edition.
resources.rationale: Follows the development of geometry from the need to be able to engineer structures that would stand to the theoretical ideas of Pythagoras and Thales.

resources.title: Student World Atlas 6th Ed.
resources.author: National Geographic
resources.maySub: other atlases; however, map work and other lessons refer specifically to this one.
resources.rationale: This atlas is used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.

resources.title: Style: The Basics of Clarity & Grace 5th ed.
resources.author: Joseph Williams, Joseph Bizup
resources.maySub: Another edition, but some content will not match exactly.
resources.rationale: This classic work covers elemental principles of writing that will help students diagnose the strengths and weaknesses of their prose quickly and revise effectively. The text is written in a conversational style, offering reason-based principles for clear writing.
resources.note: The loose-leaf edition is the most affordable and allows students to put the book directly in a composition binder. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Sufferings in Africa: The Astonishing Account of a New England Sea Captain Enslaved by North African Arabs
resources.author: Captain James Riley
resources.rationale: A true tale of shipwreck that a young Abraham Lincoln read.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Tales from Grimm
resources.author: Wanda Gag
resources.rationale: Mason had her youngest students read fairy tales from Grimm or Anderson. This retelling by Wanda Gag is gentler and less scary than many other translations, while still having good literary quality and simple English for students to practice reading themselves.

resources.title: Tales Our Abuelitas Told: A Hispanic Folktale Collection
resources.author: Alma Flor Ada & F. Isabel Campoy
resources.rationale: This is the English translation of the story collection that is used for literature lessons.

resources.title: Tales the Totems Tell
resources.author: Hugh Weatherby
resources.rationale: This excerpt contains the story of the first totem pole and the most common totem figure, the raven.
resources.note: PDF provided for member use only under Fair Use Copyright Law.

resources.title: Tasting History
resources.author: Max Miller
resources.rationale: Including the original recipe and Miller’s modern recreation, this cookbook is a must-have for any avid cook or history fan looking to experience delicious recipes from the past.

resources.title: Tecumseh
resources.author: James Laxer
resources.rationale: This book personalizes the events of the War of 1812 by presenting it through the life and experiences of Tecumseh, a Shawnee chief and close friend and ally of Major General Isaac Brock. Written by a historian of nineteenth-century Canada and biographer of Tecumseh, this book introduces readers to the important role played by Indigenous groups in the wars that shaped Canada.

resources.title: The Aeneid
resources.author: Virgil
resources.rationale: The famous epic poem in which Virgil seeks to outdo Homer and build the glory of Rome. This text complements this year's studies in ancient history which cover the Roman period.
resources.note: Excellent audiobook is also available on Amazon. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Age of Fable
resources.author: Thomas Bulfinch
resources.rationale: The Age of Fable contains mythological stories primarily from Greece and Rome, although it also includes some stories from Norse and Eastern traditions. This is a stretch book for Grades 5-6 as the reading level is more challenging than the mythology stories assigned in the earlier grades.

resources.title: The Amazing Stitching Handbook for Kids: 17 Embroidery Stitches
resources.author: Kristin Nicholas
resources.maySub: another sewing book.
resources.rationale: This book contains embroidery stitches that can be added to sewing projects.

resources.title: The Art of Asking Better Questions
resources.author: J.R. Briggs
resources.rationale: Rounding out the many dimensions of citizenship, this book will serve educators and students well throughout a lifetime of growing relationships in many directions.

resources.title: The Art of Construction
resources.author: Mario Salvadori
resources.rationale: We love that this book provides hands-on activities for exploring the principles of engineering, allowing students to dip their toes into the engineering process with everyday materials.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: The Arts: A Visual Encyclopedia
resources.author: DK
resources.rationale: We will be using this as our spine throughout the course. It is a nice overview of global art history. This book was chosen due to its miniscule amount of nudity compared to other art books geared to teens.

resources.title: The Bandit of Ashley Downs: George Muller
resources.author: Dave & Neta Jackson
resources.rationale: Through the fictionalized character of Curly Roddy, the reader is introduced to George Müller (1805-1898). Müller opened an orphanage in Bristol, England, with 30 children, but through faith and prayer never went into debt and helped over 10,000 orphans during his life.

resources.title: The Bat Scientists
resources.author: Mary Kay Carson
resources.rationale: This book provides students with a closer look at an endangered species that might be right in their neighborhood. Follow Dr. Tuttle in his tireless work to understand and protect the bat and to maintain balance in the ecosystem.

resources.title: The Bible Story Handbook
resources.author: John and Kim Walton
resources.rationale: This teacher resource from Bible scholar John Walton and his wife, Kim, provides helpful background information for teaching the Bible. In a succinct manner, they offer the biblical context, interpretational issues, and mistakes to avoid for numerous Biblical passages.
resources.note: This is a teacher resource to be read before the lesson. At times, the lesson plans may highlight certain sections. We encourage teachers to preview the selections and choose what ideas to share or discuss with their students.

resources.title: The Biology Coloring Book
resources.author: Robert D. Griffin
resources.maySub: Teachers may substitute any games or activities on the topics covered in the General Biology text.
resources.rationale: In The Biology Coloring Book, students learn about the basic processes of life and the interactions of living things within their environment.

resources.title: The Book of Indians
resources.author: Holling C. Holling
resources.rationale: Little Turtle lives on the cliffs of the desert. The other children make fun of him because he is slow and clumsy, so he plays by himself. One day, strangers approach Little Turtle's village. What Little Turtle does in response makes him a hero.
resources.note: PDF provided for member use only under Fair Use Copyright Law.

resources.title: The Book of Playground Songs & Rhymes
resources.author: John Feierabend
resources.maySub: earlier edition
resources.rationale: This book contains traditional children's songs and rhymes used to teach rhythm, jump rope, and hand-eye coordination.
resources.note: Song recordings will be available June 1.

resources.title: The Book of Song Dances
resources.author: John Feierabend
resources.maySub: earlier edition, but some dances have different page numbers.
resources.rationale: This book uses traditional songs, rhymes, and dances to teach children basic dance steps.
resources.note: Instructional videos will be available June 1.

resources.title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind
resources.author: William Kamkwamba & Bryan Mealer
resources.rationale: This wonderful story demonstrates the beauty, humanity, and accessibility of the engineering mindset.  This original version contains some sensitive content, which enriches the reader's understanding of the author's culture and challenges, but may not be appropriate for all learners.
resources.note:
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

Choose this OR The Young Reader's Edition

resources.title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind, Young Reader's Edition
resources.author: William Kamkwamba & Bryan Mealer
resources.rationale: This wonderful story demonstrates the beauty, humanity, and accessibility of the engineering mindset.
resources.note:
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

Choose this OR the standard edition

resources.title: The Bronze Bow
resources.author: Elizabeth George Speare
resources.rationale: Students will be immersed in the world of first-century Judea as they read this Newberry Award Winner. This book helps students understand the historical realities of Roman occupation and Jewish resistance. It tells the story of 18 year old Daniel bar Jamin, who seeks to avenge his father’s death and is filled with hatred toward the Romans who are beginning to invade his country of Israel. Through the friendships he makes and the words he hears spoken by Jesus of Nazareth, Daniel learns that hatred is not the answer.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation
resources.author: Deirdre Kessler
resources.rationale: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation is a detailed, engagingly-written account of the historical factors, issues, political players, and conferences which eventually led to Canada's Confederation in 1867. Attention is paid to the motives and pressures urging British North America towards unity, as well as the specific interests and context of each of the colonies before Confederation. The book brings this historical period to life with numerous photographs, paintings, archival documents, and other primary sources.

resources.title: The Chemical Elements Coloring and Activity Book
resources.author: Ellen Johnston McHenry
resources.maySub: Teachers may substitute any periodic table game/resource that they prefer, such as the board game Periodic or Theodore Gray's The Elements.
resources.rationale: All 118 elements are included in coloring pages, with information about its discovery, examples of molecules it will form, and pictures of how the element might be used in industry, medicine, the military, research, and fine arts.  Also included are a personalizable periodic table and a variety of games.

resources.title: The Colosseum
resources.author: Keith Hopkins & Mary Beard
resources.rationale: History as seen through the lens of a famous ancient building.
resources.note: Sensitive Content. Optional read for interested students.

resources.title: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering
resources.author: Valerie McKeehan
resources.rationale: This book includes lessons on various styles of lettering, in addition to project design, and adding dimension using chalk.
resources.note: Video tutorials provided with lesson plans.

resources.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
resources.author: Alexandre Dumas, translated and edited by Lowell Bair
resources.rationale: A sweeping tale of (in)justice and revenge inspired by a real-life case, this historical adventure novel became one of Alexandre Dumas's most popular works.
resources.note: Many different editions of this book exist, so if buying used be sure you are purchasing the Bantam Classics edition translated and abridged by Lowell Bair. Bair's version shortens the original work to a (somewhat) more manageable length without truncating the richness of the language and plot. If interested in reading the full text, we recommend Robin Buss's translation published by Penguin.

resources.title: The Craft of Research
resources.author: Wayne C. Booth
resources.maySub: an older edition
resources.rationale: A practical guide to research and more advanced writing.
resources.note: If subbing an older edition, some specific readings may be slightly different, but the main structure of the book has remained the same.

resources.title: The Crescent Moon
resources.author: Rabindranath Tagore
resources.rationale: This delightful collection of children's poetry was translated from Bengali into English by Tagore himself. Lesson plans will draw from this collection and include links to additional poetry by this famous 19th century author.

resources.title: The Crossroads of Science and Faith: Answers to Exercises
resources.author: Kober, Benecchi, & Gossard
resources.maySub: ebook from lovingtruthbooks.com
resources.rationale: Contains answers to exercises included in the accompanying text.

resources.title: The Crossroads of Science and Faith: Astronomy Through a Christian Worldview
resources.author: Kober, Benecchi, & Gossard
resources.maySub: ebook from lovingtruthbooks.com
resources.rationale: Astronomy text with worldview discussions, which teachers may include as desired. Students that have read any Christian apologetics, such as Mere Christianity, may enjoy engaging in their own discussion and find that they do not need the additional support, however.

resources.title: The Day Without Yesterday
resources.author: John Farrell
resources.rationale: An insightful story about the modern history of an evolving universe and how Georges Lemaitre convinced a generation of thinkers to embrace the notion of cosmic expansion.
resources.note: Available in Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: The Elements of Baking
resources.author: Katarina Cermelj
resources.rationale: This title is ideal for learning the science of baking, in which students will learn about and observe the properties of their ingredients, experiment with substitutions, and work toward their desired product.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: The Elephant Scientist
resources.author: Caitlin O'Connell and Donna M. Jackson
resources.rationale: Challenges arise when wild animals meet human communities. The scientist in this story not only observes elephants, but she also uses what she learns from them, along with what she already knows about sound waves, to collaborate with the local community to navigate such challenges.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Field Guide to Geology
resources.author: David Lambert
resources.rationale: Basic principles of geology are laid out well with helpful illustrations.

resources.title: The Fool and the Heretic
resources.author: Todd Wood & Darrel Falk
resources.maySub: another selection from the Alveary Bookshelf.
resources.rationale: Two scientists with differing perspectives dialogue respectfully about creation and evolution.  This is an excellent choice for students in contexts that perceive faith and science to be antagonistic, as well as those interested in learning to have healthy discussion with those of differing viewpoints.
resources.note:
Choose this OR The Spirit of the Rainforest.
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: The Forest in the Tree
resources.author: Ailsa Wild, Aviva Reed, Briony Barr, & Gregory Crocetti
resources.rationale: This lovely picture book brings together important botanical and ecological topics within the context of a story.

resources.title: The Frog Scientist
resources.author: Pamela Turner
resources.rationale: Scientists now know that frogs are particularly sensitive to pollution. Readers join the work of scientists who are learning just how chemical run-off affects their life cycle. This text is a particularly good example of the relationship between field and laboratory work and introduces important facets of excellent experimental design "by-the-way."
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Gift of the Great Buffalo
resources.author: Carole Lindstrom
resources.rationale: Through the story of Rose, a Métis-Ojibwe girl on the prairie in the 1860s, The Gift of the Buffalo introduces young readers to Métis culture and the vital relationship between Indigenous communities and the buffalo, which provided sustenance and shelter.

resources.title: The Girl Who Drew Butterflies: How Maria Merian's Art Changed Science
resources.author: Joyce Sidman
resources.rationale: One of the first naturalists to observe live insects directly, Maria Sibylla Merian was also one of the first to document the metamorphosis of the butterfly and the close relationships between insects and plants. In this richly illustrated nonfiction biography, readers meet the "mother" of ecology, a woman who flouted convention in the pursuit of knowledge.
resources.note: Available on Audible and Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Hands-On Equations Verbal Problems Book
resources.author: Hands-On Equations
resources.rationale: Contains word problems wherein students apply what they learned in their lessons to real-world situations.
resources.note: Must scroll down on site to purchase correct item.

resources.title: The Harvest Birds/Los pájaros de la cosecha
resources.author: Blanca Lopez de Mariscal
resources.rationale: This is a story for beginning readers in Spanish that showcases the traditions of rural Mexico.

resources.title: The Hidden Life of Trees: The Illustrated Edition
resources.author: Peter Wohlleben
resources.rationale: This title provides students with current scientific information and human connections to support their knowledge about trees both as individuals and within their context.  The beautiful photography in this piece of botany literature is inspiring and supportive.

resources.title: The Hive Detectives: Chronicle of a Honey Bee Catastrophe
resources.author: Loree Griffin Burns
resources.rationale: This book shows concepts from ecology, mathematics, and chemistry as it invites students into conversation on an important current issue.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with advanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Honour Drum
resources.author: Cheryl Bear and Tim Huff
resources.rationale: Written as a partnership between two friends--an Indigenous woman and a non-Indigenous man--this story introduces children to the history, stories, traditions, and symbols that have been meaningful to Indigenous people from the distant past right up to today. Its aim is to foster understanding, empathy, and appreciation, and to spark meaningful conversations with your children.

resources.title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
resources.author: Rebecca Skloot
resources.rationale: Biography of the woman whose stolen cells shaped the development of today's medicine.
resources.note:
Sensitive Content. 

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Joy of Movement
resources.author: Kelly McGonigal
resources.rationale: Kelly McGonigal, PhD, research psychologist, lecturer at Stanford University and award winning science writer, blends her passion for science and her talent for storytelling and illustrates to readers how movement is integral to both our happiness and our humanity. Readers walk away with insight from neuroscience, psychology, anthropology and biology to see how movement is intertwined with some of the most basic human joys and they learn why it is a powerful antidote to the modern epidemics of depression, anxiety and loneliness.

resources.title: The Kids Book of Canada
resources.author: Barbara Greenwood
resources.rationale: This book gives an engaging overview of Canada's ten provinces and three territories and includes maps, geographical features, important facts, symbols, significant people, places, and events.

resources.title: The Kids Book of Canadian History
resources.author: Carlotta Hacker
resources.rationale: This book brings Canada's history to life with engaging illustrations, helpful maps, and a narrative that keeps the complex events of our nation's past straightforward enough for even the youngest historians.

resources.title: The Last Stargazers
resources.author: Emily Levesque
resources.rationale: From the lonely quiet of midnight stargazing to tall tales of wild bears loose in the observatory, The Last Stargazers is a love letter to astronomy and an affirmation of the crucial role that humans can and must play in the future of scientific discovery.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: The LEGO® Architect
resources.author: Tom Alphin
resources.rationale: Travel through the history of architecture in _The LEGO Architect_. Learn about styles like Art Deco, Modernism, and High-Tech, and find inspiration in galleries of LEGO models. Then take a turn building 12 models in a variety of styles.

resources.title: The LEGO® Engineer
resources.author: Jeff Friesen
resources.rationale: LEGO® bricks meet _The Way Things Work_ in this fun, informative tour of the world of engineering, from the creative mind of expert LEGO® builder Jeff Friesen.

resources.title: The Life We're Looking For: Reclaiming Relationship in a Technological World
resources.author: Andy Crouch
resources.rationale: A deeply reflective primer on creating meaningful connections, rebuilding abundant communities, and living in a way that engages our full humanity in an age of unprecedented anxiety and loneliness.

resources.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
resources.author: Margarita Engle
resources.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
resources.author: C. S. Lewis
resources.maySub: another in the Narnia series or a title in adjacent grades if students have already read The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe
resources.rationale: This piece of classic literature by C.S. Lewis was chosen to foster students' moral formation and love for a well-told story.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Loxleys and Confederation
resources.author: Mark Zuehlke
resources.rationale: The story of Canada's Confederation of 1867 told in a graphic novel format. Students meet key historical figures whose debates and vision shaped the course of Canada's history. The story also introduces other significant events during the period, and includes a written afterward that serves as a historical overview.

resources.title: The Making of a Poem
resources.author: Eavan Boland, Mark Strand
resources.rationale: Students read this anthology to learn how to study the characteristics and history of various poetic forms and then enjoy a number of examples from poets across the years, including many from this year's time period.

resources.title: The Man Who Invented Christmas directed
resources.author: Bharat Nalluri
resources.rationale: Movie based on the biographical novel by Les Standiford telling how A Christmas Carol was written.

resources.title: The Map That Changed the World
resources.author: Simon Winchester
resources.rationale: A biography of a famous geologist.  This optional selection can be added to enhance the literary component of the course.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Master Theorem: A Book of Puzzles, Intrigue and Wit
resources.author: M
resources.rationale: M's Theorems are not your average Sudoku or crossword puzzles. They're each completely unique, beautifully illustrated, "escape room"-like puzzles that’ll have you decrypting messages and tackling creative brain-teasers.

resources.title: The Milkweed Lands: An Epic Story of One Plant: Its Nature and Ecology
resources.author: Eric Lee-Mader
resources.rationale: Delve into the fascinating role of this often-overlooked plant in a wide variety of ecosystems.  A collaboration between a noted ecologist and an award-winning botanical illustrator, this text will inspire readers in their own special studies.

resources.title: The Narrative of Lunsford Lane, Formerly of Raleigh, N. C.
resources.author: Lunsford Lane
resources.rationale: Originally published in 1842, this autobiography of Lunsford Lane details his life of slavery and his eventual escape using a unique method, purchasing his own freedom. To do this, he would need to have help from others around him. Lunsford Lane faced many hardships and took on entire judicial systems in his quest for his and his family’s freedom. Students, when reading The Narrative of Lunsford Lane, will encounter the real-life story of a brave man who lost his home but freed himself and his family.

resources.title: The Naturalist's Notebook
resources.author: Nathaniel T. Wheelwright & Bernd Heinrich
resources.rationale: Become a more attentive observer and deepen your appreciation for the natural world. The unique five-year calendar format of _The Naturalist’s Notebook_ helps you create a long-term record and point of comparison for memorable events.

resources.title: The New Manners and Customs of Bible Times
resources.author: Ralph Gower
resources.rationale: Through illustrations, photos, and clear explanations, this best-selling reference book shows what life was like in Bible times and makes Scripture come to life as students imagine living in that time and place. We will assign certain sections before or after Bible readings that will help students understand the context of the passage and gain more insight into the culture and geography of Israel.

resources.title: The Octopus Scientists: Exploring the Mind of a Mollusk
resources.author: Sy Montgomery
resources.rationale: This book provides students with a closer look at a creature from the family Mollusca. With respect and persistence, the author pursues the mysterious and fascinating octopus - and shares all that she learns with us, of course!
resources.note: Available on Audible and Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Pencil
resources.author: Susan Avingaq
resources.rationale: In The Pencil, the Inuk filmmaker and author Susan Avingaq recalls childhood memories from her early life in the Arctic, living in an igloo with her family. Through this joyful story, Avingaq shows the importance of good stewardship, using what we have wisely, and the delight that can be found in simple objects.

resources.title: The Plutarch Project Primer: Publicola (Revised)
resources.author: Anne White
resources.rationale: Students will read about Publicola, one of the first consuls of the Roman Republic. The Plutarch Primer includes vocabulary, discussion questions, and other aids for students and parents/teachers, plus edited text for Plutarch's Life of Publicola. It is designed especially for those who are new to the study of Plutarch. Lesson plans will include additional guidance.

resources.title: The Plutarch Project Volume Three (Revised)
resources.author: Anne White
resources.rationale: Students will read about the lives of Julius Caesar, Agis and Cleomenes, and the Gracchi. Anne White has broken up the Lives into reasonable sections with suitable omissions for length and content. She also included study helps to aid with understanding. Lesson plans will offer guidance as well.

resources.title: The Poet: Pauline Johnson
resources.author: David Robertson & Scott Henderson
resources.maySub: links to a short bio about Pauline Johnson (included in the lesson plans).
resources.rationale: A graphic novel biography of one of Canada's famous 19th century poets!

resources.title: The Prime Ministers: Canada's Leaders and the Nation They Shaped
resources.author: J.D.M. Stewart
resources.rationale: The Prime Ministers is a journey through Canada's political history through a chronological survey of its twenty-four Prime Ministers. Written specifically for high school students (and adults) to provide them with deeper knowledge of our nation's history, this book is less a collection of biographical sketches, and more a series of profiles focused on the defining events, policy decision, visions for the country, character strengths and weaknesses, missteps and triumphs, and leadership style of each Prime Minister.
resources.note: This book will be used over two years in high school

resources.title: The Reasons for Seasons
resources.author: Gail Gibbons
resources.rationale: This book is a great introduction to seasons, the earth's orbit, and axial tilt.
resources.note: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Road to There: Mapmakers and Their Stories
resources.author: Val Ross
resources.rationale: Unexpected stories of mapmakers from the current history rotation.

resources.title: The Secret Life of the Skunk
resources.author: Laurence Pringle
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about a mammal
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common but less-often-observed creature.  It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: The Sibley Guide to Trees
resources.author: David Allen Sibley
resources.rationale: Botany students use this guide to study tree families from across North America and to consider various aspects of tree anatomy.
resources.note: For local tree identification, students will still want one or more of the local/regional field guides.

resources.title: The Silk Roads: A New History of the World
resources.author: Peter Frankopan
resources.rationale: A fascinating look at the interlocking history of the East and the West.
resources.note: This book is used in several rotations and is a more advanced version of the book used last year in Grade 9. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Souls of Black Folk
resources.author: W.E.B. Dubois
resources.rationale: This landmark text of American literature and sociology is paired with Up from Slavery by Booker T. Washington in the lesson plans. This pairing immerses students in the dialogues around the turn of the century as they read about Washington's life and aspirations and then consider Dubois's response.
resources.note: Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.

resources.title: The Spirit of Canada: Canada's Story in Legends, Fiction, Poems, and Songs
resources.author: Barbara Hehner
resources.rationale: Organized thematically, this beautiful anthology encompasses a wide variety of voices and stories that reflect Canada's past and present. It is an excellent accompaniment to Canadian history and literature, as it contains many different genres: poems, songs, letters, legends, stories, folk tales, historical accounts, and more.
resources.note: Canadian students should purchase this book and NOT American Tall Tales for the Tall Tales/Fables course.

resources.title: The Spirit of the Rainforest
resources.author: Dr. Rosa Vasquez Espinoza
resources.maySub: another selection from the Alveary Bookshelf.
resources.rationale: A leading scientist explores the synergy between indigenous knowledge and classical science in this courageous and deeply spiritual adventure. This is an excellent choice for students who are secure in the harmony between faith and science and would benefit from a living example in their modern world, as well as those interested in indigenous voices.
resources.note:
Choose this OR The Fool and the Heretic.
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: The Story of Canada
resources.author: Janet Lunn, Christopher Moore
resources.maySub: previous edition. Page numbers will differ from lesson plans.
resources.rationale: Canadian history is told through rich narrative, recreations of daily life, folk tales, and fascinating facts.

resources.title: The Story of Science: Aristotle Leads the Way
resources.author: Joy Hakim
resources.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.
resources.note:
Sensitive content. Lesson plans provide support, but teachers might choose to pre-read and plan how to handle potentially controversial topics.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Story of Science: Einstein Adds a New Dimension
resources.author: Joy Hakim
resources.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.
resources.note:
Sensitive content. Lesson plans provide support, but teachers might choose to pre-read and plan how to handle potentially controversial topics.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Story of Science: Newton At the Center
resources.author: Joy Hakim
resources.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.
resources.note:
Sensitive content. Lesson plans provide support, but teachers might choose to pre-read and plan how to handle potentially controversial topics. 

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Story of the Earth in 25 Rocks
resources.author: Donald R. Prothero
resources.rationale: A fascinating look at what the rocks tell us according to a well-known expert in his field.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: The Story of the World Vol.3: Early Modern Times (Revised Edition)
resources.author: Susan Wise Bauer
resources.rationale: This book outlines significant events on every continent and includes maps, illustrations, and tales from the various cultures.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Story of the World Vol.4: The Modern Age (Revised Edition)
resources.author: Susan Wise Bauer
resources.rationale: This series offers a comprehensive overview of world history that is up-to-date and pleasant to read.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Student Bible Atlas, Revised Edition
resources.author: Tim Dowley
resources.maySub: other Bible atlas, though page numbers in lesson plans will not match.
resources.rationale: This is a simple, inexpensive Bible atlas that provides full-page maps of the biblical lands of the Old and New Testaments. In Bible lessons, students will be directed to look at specific pages. This resource is for Grades 3-8 ONLY.

resources.title: The Tales of Uncle Remus: The Adventures of Brer Rabbit
resources.author: Julius Lester
resources.rationale: Whether he is besting Brer Fox or sneaking into Mr. Man's garden, Brer Rabbit demonstrates that brains are better than brawn, along with other life lessons. These classic tales from the African American tradition are full of humor and creativity, and Julius Lester's retelling makes them a delight for readers today.

resources.title: The Tempest
resources.author: William Shakespeare
resources.rationale: This play is often called a Tragicomedy because it has both comedic and tragic elements. It is about an overthrown Duke and his daughter who are exiled on an island. The play addresses the themes of power, betrayal, magic, slavery, and the restoration of relationships.

resources.title: The Tempest (Audio recording)
resources.author: Arkangel
resources.maySub: Librivox recording

resources.title: The Things We Make
resources.author: Bill Hammack, Ph.D
resources.rationale: From medieval cathedrals to modern soda cans, this book will help learners grasp 'the engineering method' and notice it working all around them.
resources.note:
There is some mild sensitive content in Ch.2 for which support will be provided in lesson 1.  Teachers might choose to preview this chapter in advance.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

resources.title: The Tornado Scientist
resources.author: Mary Kay Carson
resources.rationale: In this captivating story, readers follow a tornado scientist, learn about her life, her work, and her passion to save lives by improving our understanding of the weather.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: The Tree is Older Than You Are: A Bilingual Gathering of Poems & Stories from Mexico with Paintings by Mexican Artists
resources.author: Naomi Shihab Nye
resources.rationale: Contains poems in Spanish and English to give students deeper insight into the culture.

resources.title: The Ultimate Homeschool Physical Education Game Book
resources.author: Guy Bailey
resources.maySub: The Physical Educator's Big Book of Sport Lead-Up Games if you have a co-op or school.
resources.rationale: This book contains time-honored and newer playground games and skill-building activities for organized sports.

resources.title: The Ultimate Sashiko Sourcebook
resources.author: Susan Briscoe
resources.rationale: Students will use the illustrations and directions in the book to learn Sashiko techniques and designs.

resources.title: The Underground Railroad: Next Stop, Toronto!
resources.author: Adrienne Shadd, Afua Cooper, & Karolyn Smardz Frost
resources.rationale: The Underground Railroad: Next Stop, Toronto! is a detailed account of Black history in Canada focusing on the stories of those who made the dangerous journey north to freedom. Using a wide variety of primary sources, including archival documents, newspaper articles, letters, photographs, maps, archeological artifacts, and more, this book recounts what happened to those freedom-seekers once they arrived safely and settled. Through this lens, readers are also introduced to what life was like in the city of Toronto in the nineteenth century. The presence and contribution of Black people who made their homes in Toronto contributed significantly to the history and development of the city.

resources.title: The Universe in You: A Microscopic Journey
resources.author: Jason Chin
resources.rationale: This stunning picture book introduces students to Things-too-small-to-see.  With beautiful artwork and accessible language, this title is sure to inspire everyone to look more closely.

resources.title: The Unweaving
resources.author: Cheryl Perisien
resources.rationale: The Red River Resistance of 1869 (also known as the First Riel Rebellion) is brought to life through the experiences of one Métis family in this work of historical fiction. Unfolding in four perspectives, this novel explores how the turmoil and the conflict affect each member of the family differently. The narrative weaves historical events and people (including the author's own ancestor) into the lives of this one family, and it is a thoroughly researched, richly detailed, and beautifully written account of the great upheaval faced by the Red River Colony in the years after Confederation.

resources.title: The Very First Americans
resources.author: Cara Ashrose & Bryna Waldman
resources.rationale: This book gives a brief overview of how humans came to North America and how the very diverse people groups lived.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: The Virtual Instructor (Art)
resources.rationale: This is a wonderful art teacher resource for students.

resources.title: The Way Things Work: Newly Revised Edition: The Ultimate Guide to How Things Work
resources.author: David Macaulay
resources.maySub: other editions
resources.rationale: This well-written and humorously illustrated classic guides readers through the fundamental principles of machines and shows how the developments of the past are building blocks of the world of tomorrow.

resources.title: The Winter's Tale
resources.author: William Shakespeare
resources.rationale: The Winter's Tale is play divided into two halves—tragedy and redemption. It begins in the court of Leontes, King of Sicilia, whose sudden and irrational jealousy leads him to accuse his wife Hermione of betrayal. His actions cause devastating consequences, including the loss of family, friendship, and trust. This play emphasizes the power of jealousy to destroy, but the greater power of love to restore.

resources.title: The Winter's Tale (Audio recording)
resources.author: Arkangel
resources.maySub: Librivox recording

resources.title: The Witness of Early Christian Women: Mothers of the Church
resources.author: Mike Aquilina
resources.rationale: Source documents/writings alongside short biographies of women and their impact in the early church.

resources.title: The Wolves and Moose of Isle Royale: Restoring an Island Ecosystem
resources.author: Nancy F. Castaldo
resources.rationale: Students discover the threatened predator-prey relationship that maintains the Isle Royale ecosystem in this fascinating story. Will scientists be able to restore the balance?  Let's follow this living laboratory experiment.

resources.title: The Word Made Fresh
resources.author: Abram Van Engen
resources.rationale: Van Engen lays out a beautiful relational approach to poetry which resonates deeply with Mason. As he models a love for poetry and gives insights into how to enjoy, understand, and read poetry (or start on the journey!), students will continue growing their own delight. Lesson plans pair readings from the book with practical exercises.
resources.note: The first section of the book (the more practical part) is scheduled in the lesson plans, but we recommend the whole book - which concludes with a theological reflection on the value of poetry - as a resource for parents and teachers.

resources.title: They Fought Like Demons: Women Soldiers in the Civil War
resources.author: De Anne Blanton & Lauren M. Cook
resources.rationale: Most people will never know the truth behind people like Albert Cashier, Frances Clayton, or the countless other aliases that women took on so that they could join the fight for causes that they believed in. The authors dive into military archives with the goal to uncover the many stories of women who stepped into the role of a male soldier in the 1800s. And with engaging chapters and well-rounded research, the student will be able to see the war through the eyes of women as they took to the battlefield alongside brothers, sons, fathers, and husbands. These women would come to be known later on—courtesy of documents from soldiers—as soldiers who fought like demons.

resources.title: Things Fall Apart
resources.author: Chinua Achebe
resources.rationale: Widely considered to be the foundational text of modern African fiction, Things Fall Apart provides a detailed, unflinching depiction of the complex life and beliefs of the Igbo tribe of Nigeria in the late 1800s, before and after their first encounters with European missionaries and the establishment of a colonial government. The story traces the life of the warrior Okonkwo: his upbringing and adulthood, his rise to prominence and and tragic fall, and finally his banishment and return to find his home forever changed. Written in English from an authentically African perspective, Achebe's novel directly challenges Western narratives of colonialism and stereotypes of Africa, yet eschews any simple resolutions.
resources.note: Recommended for mature Grades 11-12 students due to sensitive subject matter. Students who might need a lighter read may choose a title from the Grades 9-10 literature selections.

resources.title: Think: The Life of the Mind and the Love of God
resources.author: John Piper
resources.rationale: A focus on using both heart and mind to know and love God and care for others.
resources.note: Choose this OR Atomic Habits

resources.title: Thirteen Moons on Turtle's Back: A Native American Year of Moons
resources.author: Joseph Bruchac & Jonathan London
resources.rationale: In Native American legend, the thirteen scales on a turtle's back symbolize the annual thirteen cycles of the moon and the seasonal changes each one brings. The thirteen poems in this book -- one for each moon of the year, and each from a different indigenous group -- celebrate the natural world and weave together traditional knowledge and folklore. Each poem is illustrated by a beautiful painting.

resources.title: This Changes Everything
resources.author: Jaquelle Ferris
resources.rationale: This book speaks directly to teenagers, showing how the gospel shapes identity, purpose, and everyday choices. The author challenges the idea that faith is something to “grow into later” and instead invites young readers to take their beliefs seriously now.

resources.title: This Strange Wilderness: The Life and Art of John James Audubon
resources.author: Nancy Plain
resources.rationale: It tells the story of Audubon’s (1785-1851) life as a naturalist, artist, and explorer in early America. The book follows his journeys through forests, swamps, and frontier settlements as he studied and painted North American birds in their natural habitats.

resources.title: Thomas Day: Master Craftsman and Free Man of Color
resources.author: Patricia Marshall
resources.rationale: Furniture and architecture from the time period

resources.title: To Fly: The Story of the Wright Brothers
resources.author: Wendie Old
resources.maySub: First to Fly by Peter Busby
resources.rationale: This book tells the story of an important technology that students have probably observed and maybe even used. Along the way, they learn how the Wright Brothers were driven by curiosity and inspiration and how they persisted to solve one of the biggest challenges of their day.

resources.title: Tracking Trash
resources.author: Loree Griffin Burns
resources.rationale: Students follow a scientist who uses creative methods to better understand ocean currents and our impact on life within the sea.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: Transformed by Truth
resources.author: Katherine Forster
resources.rationale: This book is written for students to encourage them to make Bible reading a special time of their day. The author demonstrates the practices of observation, interpretation, and application so students can discover how God's truth can literally transform them from the inside out. These practices will also be implemented during the NT Bible lessons.

resources.title: Tree Finder - Eastern US
resources.author: May Watts
resources.rationale: Regional guide
resources.note: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

resources.title: Trial by Poison: Mary Slessor
resources.author: Dave & Neta Jackson
resources.rationale: Born in Scotland, Mary Slessor (1848-1915) became a missionary to Calabar, Africa (now Southern Nigeria) at the age of 27. As she moved further inland, she brought the gospel to unreached people and rescued many twin babies, who otherwise would have been killed because of the mistaken beliefs of their parents.

resources.title: Turning Points in American Church History
resources.author: Elesha J. Coffman
resources.rationale: An insightful look at some key moments leading up to, during, and following our current history rotation to give more context and perspective on how these events have impacted the American church.
resources.note: This book will be used in other history rotations.

resources.title: Typing.com
resources.rationale: This program uses fun activities and games to teach keyboarding fluency.
resources.note: Create a free account.

resources.title: Under the Hawthorn Tree
resources.author: Marita Conlon-McKenna
resources.rationale: This book is a fictional account of a family during the 1840s when Ireland was devastated by the potato famine. Although it is a dark time in history, this story also shows the resilience of the three siblings, who continue to care for one another despite hunger, fear, and constant uncertainty. This is an important event that helps students understand why so many Irish immigrants came to the US during this time period.

resources.title: United States Atlas 7th Ed.
resources.author: National Geographic Kids
resources.maySub: earlier edition. Other atlases may be used; however, map work and other lessons refer specifically to this one.
resources.rationale: This atlas is used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.
resources.note: Optional for Grade 1. For U.S. students.

resources.title: Up from Slavery
resources.author: Booker T. Washington
resources.rationale: This is a first-person account of what it was like to be born on a slave plantation, live through the Civil War and Reconstruction, and make a new life as a free person. Washington's accomplishments and sense of hope are inspiring, and his founding of the Tuskegee Institute which continues as a historically black university and National Historic Site today is a remarkable achievement. Lesson plans pair Dubois's response to Washington to allow students a richer experience of the discussions of the times.
resources.note:
Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.

Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: Up in the Garden and Down in the Dirt
resources.author: Kate Messner
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book about garden habitats as preferred by the teacher.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes us on a trip to the garden alongside a girl and her grandmother. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
resources.note: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

resources.title: US Wall Map (US students only)
resources.author: National Geographic
resources.maySub: any US wall map that shows both geographical features and place names. Avoid maps with roadways, as these obscure other features.
resources.rationale: This map clearly shows landforms, and its projection is more accurate than some other maps.
resources.note: Hang map at student eye level.

resources.title: Van Gogh
resources.author: Ingo Walther & Rainer Metzger
resources.rationale: This comprehensive study of Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890) pairs a detailed monograph on his life and art with a complete catalogue of his 871 paintings.
resources.note: Please note there is some minor nudity, and Van Gogh lived a tumultuous life, ultimately committing suicide.

resources.title: Vanished Beyond the Map
resources.author: Adam Shoalts
resources.rationale: Vanished Beyond the Map unfolds like a mystery novel: after first stumbling upon the name Hubert Darrell in an old encyclopedia, Adam Shoalts sets off on a journey through the arctic and the archives to discover as much as he can about this nearly-forgotten explorer, mapmaker and guide who disappeared in 1910. This book is remarkable for showing readers what is involved in trying to solve a historical mystery: where do you find clues? what kinds of questions do you ask? how much can we really know about the past? But it is also a book which situates Darrell within the wider world of geographers and explorers in the late-nineteenth century, who were attempting to complete the map of the world by charting the unknown lands of the arctic and antarctic.
resources.note: This book is assigned for both Geography: Grade 8 (Canada) and History: Grade 9-10 (Canada). Note the possible overlap if you are combining students in either of these courses.

resources.title: Walks with a Naturalist: Living Nature Reader Volume 1
resources.author: Melissa Dowland
resources.maySub: another nature reader that provides accurate, living ideas across a variety of creatures; supplemental links and prompts may not apply to substituted reader
resources.rationale: Our nature reader brings learners alongside an experienced nature educator as she relates her stories with curiosity, delight, and humility.  Volume 1 explores insects, spiders, birds, seeds, and more.

resources.title: Walks with a Naturalist: Living Nature Reader Volume 2
resources.author: Melissa Dowland
resources.maySub: another nature reader that provides accurate, living ideas across a variety of creatures; supplemental links and prompts may not apply to substituted reader
resources.rationale: Our nature reader brings learners alongside an experienced nature educator as she relates her stories with curiosity, delight, and humility.  Volume 2 explores insects, insect larvae, mammals, some aquatic species, and more.

resources.title: Westward with Fremont: The Story of Solomon Carvalho
resources.author: Sophie Greenspan
resources.rationale: This book tells the true story of Solomon Nunes Carvalho, a Jewish artist and early photographer who joined explorer John C. Frémont on a dangerous expedition across the American West in the 1850s.  Students will come to see how maps were made from firsthand observation and why accurate geographic knowledge mattered for exploration and expansion. It is a fascinating read that follows the challenges of exploration—extreme weather, lack of supplies, illness, and difficult terrain.
resources.note: Use option one link to receive discount.

resources.title: What a Plant Knows
resources.author: Daniel Chamovitz
resources.rationale: A plant knows more than you think! This piece of botany literature provides a fascinating look at plants.
resources.note: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

resources.title: What Is Poetry?
resources.author: Michael Rosen
resources.rationale: This book is full of interesting ideas about poetry. The style is wonderfully readable and engaging and takes away the intimidation factor when it comes to both reading and writing poetry.

resources.title: What Makes Day and Night
resources.author: Franklyn M. Branley
resources.rationale: Through diagrams, pictures, and simple text, this book helps students gain a better understanding of how the rotation of the earth gives us day and night.
resources.note: Teachers may wish to check this book out from the library rather than purchase it, as it is only used for a couple of lessons. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

resources.title: What Would Keynes Do?
resources.author: Tejvan Pettinger
resources.rationale: An easy-to-follow introduction to economics and how it applies to real-life decisions from a variety of economic theory approaches.
resources.note: Used in grades 10 & 11.

resources.title: When America First Met China
resources.author: Eric Jay Dolin
resources.rationale: This is the story of trade between nations. One nation has just been born from a bloody battle with Britain, and one nation is steeped in a 2000-year tradition. The United States of America has to break away from using its mother nation for trading, and as it navigates trade with China, there will be collisions, barriers, and failings, but there will also be triumphs. China’s culture is also explored in great depth as Dolin uses stories of real people who broke the rules and recounted all the different happenings taking place in this nation.

resources.title: When the Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers
resources.author: Marcellino D'Ambrosio
resources.rationale: The first half of the book contains biographies from early church history covered in this rotation.
resources.note: ONLY Grades 10-12. Used over two years.

resources.title: Who Runs This Country, Anyway?
resources.author: Joanne Stanbridge
resources.rationale: Who Runs This Country, Anyway is a fun, lighthearted, yet thorough introduction to the structure of Canada's government, its elected officials and the election process, the Canadian Constitution and its history, the important buildings and institutions found on Parliament Hill in Ottawa, and how bills become laws.
resources.note: Look for a used copy and check your local library. Otherwise, there is a link to purchase a subscription to the audiobook version with images and notes, recorded by Donna Ward.

resources.title: Why Buildings Stand Up
resources.author: Mario Salvadori
resources.rationale: Used to give a solid and interesting introduction to building principles.  The history of architecture is explored along with the technology that explains why the buildings stand up. The author is passionate about his subject and desires his readers to love beautiful buildings, too.  He writes about architecture as the successful marriage of art and technology.

resources.title: Will Kemp Art School
resources.author: Will Kemp
resources.rationale: This is a wonderful acrylic and oil art teacher resource for students.

resources.title: William Wilberforce: The Freedom Fighter
resources.author: Derick Bingham
resources.rationale: This biography tells about the man who dedicated his life to ending the slave trade and slavery in the British Empire. The book follows the life of William Wilberforce (1759-1833) beginning with his days as a school boy to being a member in Parliament who risked being thought a fool for standing by his principles. Students will not only learn about this man of God but also learn about what was happening in England during this time period.

resources.title: Winter Sleep: A Hibernation Story
resources.author: Sean Taylor
resources.maySub: any life-giving picture book about a winter habitat preferred by the teacher.
resources.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes readers on a winter walk with a boy and his grandmother. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

resources.title: Winter Tree Finder (Eastern US)
resources.author: May Watts & Tom Watts
resources.rationale: Regional guide
resources.note: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

resources.title: Wood Shop: Handy Skills and Creative Building Projects for Kids
resources.author: Margaret Larson
resources.rationale: With step-by-step photographs and clear instructions, aspiring woodworkers learn essential skills, such as how to drive a nail; use a power drill; “measure twice, cut once;” and saw correctly.

resources.title: Woodshop 101 For Kids
resources.author: Craig Stevens
resources.rationale: Twenty-one woodworking lessons that teach the basics of woodworking.

resources.title: World Wall Map
resources.author: National Geographic
resources.maySub: any world wall map that shows both geographical features and place names
resources.rationale: The projection of this wall map distorts the image less than others. It also makes geographical features easy to find and place names easy to read.
resources.note: Hang map at student eye level.

resources.title: Your Rugged Constitution
resources.author: Bruce and Esther Findlay
resources.rationale: This book guides students through the text of the Constitution with descriptions that are put in clear, easy-to-understand language, accompanied by commentary and lively drawings so all the ideas and concepts can be easily grasped.
